correctText: BLANK1 = AddPhraseListAsync; BLANK2 = PhraselistCreateObject. Code: client.Features.AddPhraseListAsync(appId, versionId, new PhraselistCreateObject{...}).
explanation: The LUIS authoring SDK adds a phrase list feature with Features.AddPhraseListAsync, passing a PhraselistCreateObject that defines Name, Phrases, IsExchangeable and EnabledForAllModels.

question:
You plan to use a Language Understanding application named app1 deployed to a container. App1 was developed using a Language Understanding authoring resource named lu1. App1 has these versions:
Version | Trained date | Published date
V1.2 | None | None
V1.1 | 2020-10-01 | None
V1.0 | 2020-09-01 | 2020-09-15
You need to create a container that uses the latest deployable version of app1. Which three actions should you perform in sequence?

Actions: Run a container that has version set as an environment variable; Export the model by using the Export as JSON option; Select v1.1 of app1; Run a container and mount the model file; Select v1.0 of app1; Export the model by using the Export for containers (GZIP) option; Select v1.2 of app1.
order: Select v1.1 of app1
Export the model by using the Export for containers (GZIP) option
Run a container and mount the model file
correctText: 1) Select v1.1 of app1; 2) Export the model by using the Export for containers (GZIP) option; 3) Run a container and mount the model file.
explanation: V1.2 is not trained (not deployable) and V1.0 is older, so V1.1 is the latest deployable (trained) version. LUIS containers require exporting with the 'Export for containers (GZIP)' option and mounting the model file when running the container.

question: You need to build a chatbot that meets the following requirements: supports chit-chat, knowledge base, and multilingual models; performs sentiment analysis on user messages; selects the best language model automatically. What should you integrate into the chatbot?
options: A. QnA Maker, Language Understanding, and Dispatch
C. Language Understanding, Text Analytics, and QnA Maker
D. Text Analytics, Translator, and Dispatch
correctText: C. Language Understanding, Text Analytics, and QnA Maker
explanation: QnA Maker provides the knowledge base and chit-chat, Text Analytics provides sentiment analysis, and Language Understanding (with multilingual models) handles intent and automatic best-model selection.

question: Your company wants to reduce how long it takes for employees to log receipts in expense reports. All the receipts are in English. You need to extract top-level information from the receipts, such as the vendor and the transaction total. The solution must minimize development effort. Which Azure service should you use?
correctText: C. Form Recognizer
explanation: Form Recognizer (Document Intelligence) has a prebuilt receipt model that extracts vendor, total and other fields with no training, minimizing development effort.

correctText: Method = PUT; kind = CognitiveServices.
explanation: A multi-service 'CognitiveServices' (kind) resource provides a single key/endpoint and consolidated billing across services. PUT is used to create the resource in the ARM REST API.
mlTitle: Create a multi-service Azure AI services resource

question: You are developing a new sales system that will process video and text from a public-facing website. You plan to monitor the sales system to ensure that it provides equitable results regardless of the user's location or background. Which two responsible AI principles provide guidance to meet the monitoring requirements? (Choose two.)
correctText: B. fairness and C. inclusiveness
explanation: Equitable results regardless of location or background relate to fairness (treating all people fairly) and inclusiveness (empowering everyone regardless of background).

question:
You plan to use containerized versions of the Anomaly Detector API on local devices for testing and in on-premises datacenters. Requirements: prevent billing and API information from being stored in command-line histories of the devices that run the container; control access to the container images by using Azure RBAC. Which four actions should you perform in sequence? (More than one order may be correct.)

Actions: Create a custom Dockerfile; Pull the Anomaly Detector container image; Distribute a docker run script; Push the image to an Azure container registry; Build the image; Push the image to Docker Hub.
order: Create a custom Dockerfile
Pull the Anomaly Detector container image
Build the image
Push the image to an Azure container registry
correctText: 1) Create a custom Dockerfile; 2) Pull the Anomaly Detector container image; 3) Build the image; 4) Push the image to an Azure container registry.
explanation: Baking billing/API settings into a custom image (Dockerfile, pull base image, build) keeps secrets out of CLI history, and pushing to an Azure Container Registry (not Docker Hub) lets you control access with Azure RBAC.
mlTitle: Azure AI containers - build and reuse images

question:
You plan to deploy a containerized Azure Cognitive Services service for text analysis. You configure https://contoso.cognitiveservices.azure.com as the endpoint URI and pull the latest Text Analytics Sentiment Analysis container. You need to run the container on an Azure VM by using Docker. Complete the command:
docker run --rm -it -p 5000:5000 --memory 8g --cpus 1 \
[IMAGE] \
Eula=accept \
Billing=[BILLING] \
ApiKey=xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
Image options: http://contoso.blob.core.windows.net, https://contoso.cognitiveservices.azure.com, mcr.microsoft.com/azure-cognitive-services/textanalytics/keyphrase, mcr.microsoft.com/azure-cognitive-services/textanalytics/sentiment. Billing options: same four.
correctText: Image = mcr.microsoft.com/azure-cognitive-services/textanalytics/sentiment; Billing = https://contoso.cognitiveservices.azure.com
explanation: The container image to run is the sentiment image from MCR, and the Billing parameter must point to the Cognitive Services endpoint URI (https://contoso.cognitiveservices.azure.com).

question: You have the C# method create_resource(client, resource_name, kind, account_tier, location) for creating Azure Cognitive Services resources programmatically. You need to call the method to create a free Azure resource in the West US region that will be used to generate captions of images automatically. Which code should you use?
correctText: A. create_resource(client, "res1", "ComputerVision", "F0", "westus")
explanation: Image captioning uses the Computer Vision service, and the free pricing tier is F0.

question:
You successfully run the following HTTP request:
POST https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/.../resourceGroups/RG1/providers/Microsoft.CognitiveServices/accounts/contoso1/regenerateKey?api-version=2017-04-18
Body {"keyName": "Key2"}
What is the result of the request?
options: A. A key for Azure Cognitive Services was generated in Azure Key Vault.
B. A new query key was generated.
C. The primary subscription key and the secondary subscription key were rotated.
D. The secondary subscription key was reset.
correctText: D. The secondary subscription key was reset.
explanation: regenerateKey with keyName 'Key2' regenerates (resets) only the secondary subscription key.

question:
You build a custom Form Recognizer model. You receive sample files to use for training as shown:
Name | Type | Size
File1 | PDF | 20 MB
File2 | MP4 | 100 MB
File3 | JPG | 20 MB
File4 | PDF | 100 MB
File5 | GIF | 1 MB
File6 | JPG | 40 MB
Which three files can you use to train the model? (Each correct answer presents a complete solution.)
correctText: A. File1, C. File3, F. File6
explanation: Form Recognizer supports PDF, JPG, PNG and TIFF (not MP4 video or GIF) and the file size must be within the limit (50 MB for the free tier). File1 (PDF 20MB), File3 (JPG 20MB) and File6 (JPG 40MB) qualify; File4 is a 100MB PDF (too large), File2 is MP4, File5 is GIF.
mlTitle: Document Intelligence custom models - input requirements

question: A customer uses Azure Cognitive Search. The customer plans to enable server-side encryption and use customer-managed keys (CMK) stored in Azure. What are three implications of the planned change? (Each correct answer presents a complete solution.)
options: A. The index size will increase.
C. A self-signed X.509 certificate is required.
D. The index size will decrease.
F. Azure Key Vault is required.
correctText: A. The index size will increase, B. Query times will increase, F. Azure Key Vault is required.
explanation: Enabling CMK encryption in Azure Cognitive Search requires Azure Key Vault and adds overhead, which increases index size and query times.
mlTitle: Encrypt data with customer-managed keys in Azure AI Search

question: You are developing a new sales system that will process video and text from a public-facing website. You plan to notify users that their data has been processed by the sales system. Which responsible AI principle does this help meet?
correctText: A. transparency
explanation: Notifying users that their data has been processed supports the transparency principle (systems should be understandable and their use disclosed).

question: You create a web app named app1 that runs on an Azure VM named vm1. vm1 is on an Azure virtual network named vnet1. You plan to create a new Azure Cognitive Search service named service1. You need to ensure that app1 can connect directly to service1 without routing traffic over the public internet. Solution: You deploy service1 and a public endpoint to a new virtual network, and you configure Azure Private Link. Does this meet the goal?
correctText: B. No
explanation: Azure Private Link requires a private endpoint, not a public endpoint, so deploying a public endpoint does not meet the goal.
mlTitle: Create a private endpoint for Azure AI Search

question: You create a web app named app1 that runs on an Azure VM named vm1 on virtual network vnet1. You plan to create a new Azure Cognitive Search service named service1. You need to ensure app1 can connect directly to service1 without routing traffic over the public internet. Solution: You deploy service1 and a public endpoint, and you configure an IP firewall rule. Does this meet the goal?
correctText: B. No
explanation: An IP firewall rule restricts which IPs may access the public endpoint but traffic still traverses the public internet, so it does not meet the goal.
mlTitle: Create a private endpoint for Azure AI Search

question: You create a web app named app1 that runs on an Azure VM named vm1 on virtual network vnet1. You plan to create a new Azure Cognitive Search service named service1. You need to ensure app1 can connect directly to service1 without routing traffic over the public internet. Solution: You deploy service1 and a public endpoint, and you configure a network security group (NSG) for vnet1. Does this meet the goal?
correctText: B. No
explanation: An NSG filters network traffic but does not provide private connectivity; traffic to a public endpoint still uses the public internet.
mlTitle: Create a private endpoint for Azure AI Search

question: You plan to perform predictive maintenance. You collect IoT sensor data from 100 industrial machines for a year. Each machine has 50 sensors generating data at one-minute intervals. In total, you have 5,000 time series datasets. You need to identify unusual values in each time series to help predict machinery failures. Which Azure service should you use?
correctText: A. Anomaly Detector
explanation: Anomaly Detector identifies unusual values (anomalies) in time series data, which is ideal for predictive maintenance.

correctText: DROPDOWN1 = AudioStreamFormat.GetCompressedFormat; DROPDOWN2 = SpeechRecognizer
explanation: Compressed/MP3 input uses AudioStreamFormat.GetCompressedFormat to define the audio format, and speech-to-text is performed by a SpeechRecognizer.
mlTitle: Use compressed audio input formats (Speech SDK)

question:
You are developing an internet-based training solution for remote learners. Some learners leave their desk for long periods or become distracted. You need to use a video and audio feed from each learner's computer to detect whether the learner is present and paying attention. The solution must minimize development effort and identify each learner. Which Azure Cognitive Services service should you use for each requirement? (Options for each: Face, Speech, Text Analytics)
1. From a learner's video feed, verify whether the learner is present.
2. From a learner's facial expression in the video feed, verify whether the learner is paying attention.
3. From a learner's audio feed, detect whether the learner is talking.
correctText: 1) Face; 2) Face; 3) Speech
explanation: Face service verifies presence and identifies each learner and reads facial expressions (attention); Speech service detects whether the learner is talking from the audio feed.

question: You plan to provision a QnA Maker service in a new resource group named RG1. In RG1, you create an App Service plan named AP1. Which two Azure resources are automatically created in RG1 when you provision the QnA Maker service? (Choose two.)
correctText: D. Azure Cognitive Search and E. Azure App Service
explanation: Provisioning QnA Maker automatically creates an Azure Cognitive Search service (to store the knowledge base) and an Azure App Service (the runtime), plus Application Insights.
mlTitle: Set up a QnA Maker service

question: You are building a language model by using a Language Understanding (classic) service. You create a new Language Understanding (classic) resource. You need to add more contributors. What should you use?
options: A. a conditional access policy in Azure Active Directory (Azure AD)
B. the Access control (IAM) page for the authoring resources in the Azure portal
C. the Access control (IAM) page for the prediction resources in the Azure portal
correctText: B. the Access control (IAM) page for the authoring resources in the Azure portal
explanation: Contributors who author/edit the LUIS app are managed via Azure RBAC on the authoring resource's Access control (IAM) page.
mlTitle: Add contributors to a LUIS app

question: You have an Azure Cognitive Search service. During the past 12 months, query volume steadily increased. Some search query requests are being throttled. You need to reduce the likelihood that search query requests are throttled. Solution: You migrate to a Cognitive Search service that uses a higher tier. Does this meet the goal?
correctText: A. Yes
explanation: A higher tier provides more capacity (replicas/partitions and resources), which reduces query throttling.

question:
You need to develop an automated call handling system that can respond to callers in their own language. The system will support only French and English. Which Azure Cognitive Services service should you use to meet each requirement? (Drag services to requirements.)
Requirement 1: Detect the incoming language.
Requirement 2: Respond in the callers' own language.
Services: Speech to Text, Speech to Speech (translation), Text to Speech, Translator.
correctText: Detect the incoming language = Speech to Text; Respond in the callers' own language = Text to Speech.
explanation: Speech to Text can detect/transcribe the incoming spoken language, and Text to Speech generates the spoken response in the caller's language.

question: You have receipts that are accessible from a URL. You need to extract data from the receipts by using Form Recognizer and the SDK. The solution must use a prebuilt model. Which client and method should you use?
options: A. the FormRecognizerClient client and the StartRecognizeContentFromUri method
B. the FormTrainingClient client and the StartRecognizeContentFromUri method
C. the FormRecognizerClient client and the StartRecognizeReceiptsFromUri method
D. the FormTrainingClient client and the StartRecognizeReceiptsFromUri method
correctText: C. the FormRecognizerClient client and the StartRecognizeReceiptsFromUri method
explanation: FormRecognizerClient performs analysis (FormTrainingClient is for training), and StartRecognizeReceiptsFromUri invokes the prebuilt receipt model on a URL.

question: You have a collection of 50,000 scanned documents that contain text. You plan to make the text available through Azure Cognitive Search. You need to configure an enrichment pipeline to perform OCR and text analytics. The solution must minimize costs. What should you attach to the skillset?
options: A. a new Computer Vision resource
B. a free (Limited enrichments) Cognitive Services resource
C. an Azure Machine Learning Designer pipeline
D. a new Cognitive Services resource that uses the S0 pricing tier
correctText: D. a new Cognitive Services resource that uses the S0 pricing tier
explanation: Built-in AI enrichment beyond 20 documents/day requires attaching a billable multi-service Cognitive Services (S0) resource; the free option is limited to 20 documents.
mlTitle: Attach a Cognitive Services resource to a skillset

question: You have an Azure Cognitive Search service. Query volume steadily increased and some query requests are being throttled. You need to reduce the likelihood that search query requests are throttled. Solution: You add indexes. Does this meet the goal?
correctText: B. No
explanation: Adding indexes does not increase query capacity; adding replicas is what increases query throughput and reduces throttling.

question: You have an Azure Cognitive Search service. Query volume steadily increased and some query requests are being throttled. You need to reduce the likelihood that search query requests are throttled. Solution: You enable customer-managed key (CMK) encryption. Does this meet the goal?
correctText: B. No
explanation: CMK encryption adds overhead and actually increases query times; it does not reduce throttling.

question: You create a web app named app1 that runs on an Azure VM named vm1 on virtual network vnet1. You plan to create a new Azure Cognitive Search service named service1. You need to ensure app1 can connect directly to service1 without routing traffic over the public internet. Solution: You deploy service1 and a private endpoint to vnet1. Does this meet the goal?
correctText: A. Yes
explanation: A private endpoint on vnet1 provides private connectivity to the search service over the Microsoft backbone without using the public internet.
mlTitle: Create a private endpoint for Azure AI Search

question: You have a Language Understanding resource named lu1. You build and deploy an Azure bot named bot1 that uses lu1. You need to ensure that bot1 adheres to the Microsoft responsible AI principle of inclusiveness. How should you extend bot1?
options: B. Enable active learning for lu1.
C. Host lu1 in a container.
D. Add Direct Line Speech to bot1.
correctText: D. Add Direct Line Speech to bot1.
explanation: Inclusiveness means empowering everyone, including people with disabilities. Adding Direct Line Speech enables voice interaction, making the bot accessible to more users.
mlTitle: Connect a bot to Direct Line Speech

question:
You are building an app that will process incoming email and direct messages to either French or English language support teams. Which Azure Cognitive Services API should you use? Complete the request:
https://[HOST][PATH]
Host options: api.cognitive.microsofttranslator.com, eastus.api.cognitive.microsoft.com, portal.azure.com. Path options: /text/analytics/v3.1/entities/recognition/general, /text/analytics/v3.1/languages, /translator/text/v3.0/translate?to=en, /translator/text/v3.0/translate?to=fr.
correctText: Host = eastus.api.cognitive.microsoft.com; Path = /text/analytics/v3.1/languages
explanation: Routing email to French or English teams requires detecting the language, which uses the Text Analytics language-detection endpoint (/text/analytics/v3.1/languages) on the regional Cognitive Services endpoint.

question: You have an Azure Cognitive Search instance that indexes purchase orders by using Form Recognizer. You need to analyze the extracted information by using Microsoft Power BI. The solution must minimize development effort. What should you add to the indexer?
correctText: B. a table projection
explanation: Table projections in a knowledge store store enriched data as tables that Power BI can connect to and analyze with minimal effort.

question: You have an Azure Cognitive Search service. Query volume steadily increased and some query requests are being throttled. You need to reduce the likelihood that search query requests are throttled. Solution: You add replicas. Does this meet the goal?
correctText: A. Yes
explanation: Replicas increase query throughput (queries per second) and high availability, which reduces query throttling.

question: SIMULATION (hands-on lab, no multiple-choice options): You need to create a Text Analytics service named Text12345678, and then enable logging for Text12345678. The solution must ensure that any changes to Text12345678 will be stored in a Log Analytics workspace. To complete this task, sign in to the Azure portal.
correctText: Create the Text Analytics (Language) resource named Text12345678, then in the resource open Diagnostic settings, add a diagnostic setting that sends logs/metrics (e.g. Audit/AllMetrics) to a Log Analytics workspace.
explanation: Hands-on simulation: diagnostic (Azure Monitor) logging for an Azure AI services resource is configured under Diagnostic settings and routed to a Log Analytics workspace.
mlTitle: Enable diagnostic logging for Azure AI services

question: SIMULATION (hands-on lab, no multiple-choice options): You need to create a search service named search12345678 that will index a sample Azure Cosmos DB database named hotels-sample. The solution must ensure that only English language fields are retrievable. To complete this task, sign in to the Azure portal.
correctText: Create the Azure AI Search service search12345678, use Import data to connect to the Cosmos DB hotels-sample, and in the index mark only the English-language fields as Retrievable (clear Retrievable on non-English fields).
explanation: Hands-on simulation: control which fields are returned by toggling the 'Retrievable' attribute on index fields during the import/index definition.
mlTitle: Index data from Azure Cosmos DB

question: SIMULATION (hands-on lab, no multiple-choice options): You plan to create a solution to generate captions for images read from Azure Blob Storage. You need to create a service in Azure Cognitive Services for the solution. The service must be named captions12345678 and must use the Free pricing tier. To complete this task, sign in to the Azure portal.
correctText: Create a Computer Vision resource named captions12345678 on the Free (F0) pricing tier.
explanation: Hands-on simulation: image captioning uses Computer Vision; create the resource with the Free (F0) tier and the specified name.

question: SIMULATION (hands-on lab, no multiple-choice options): You need to create a Form Recognizer resource named fr12345678. Use the Form Recognizer sample labeling tool at https://fott-2-1.azurewebsites.net/ to analyze the invoice located in the C:\Resources\Invoices folder. Save the results as C:\Resources\Invoices\Results.json. To complete this task, sign in to the Azure portal and open the Form Recognizer sample labeling tool.
correctText: Create the Form Recognizer resource fr12345678, then in the sample labeling tool use the prebuilt Invoice analyzer with the resource endpoint and key to analyze the invoice and save the output JSON.
explanation: Hands-on simulation: provision the Form Recognizer (Document Intelligence) resource and use the prebuilt invoice model in the sample labeling tool to extract and save the results.

question: You have a factory that produces food products. You need to build a monitoring solution for staff compliance with personal protective equipment (PPE) requirements. Requirements: identify staff who have removed masks or safety glasses; perform a compliance check every 15 minutes; minimize development effort; minimize costs. Which service should you use?
options: C. Azure Video Analyzer for Media (formerly Video Indexer)
correctText: A. Face
explanation: The Face service can detect facial attributes/accessories such as masks and glasses, making it the lowest-effort, lowest-cost option for periodic PPE compliance checks.

question: You have an Azure Cognitive Search solution and a collection of blog posts that include a category field. You need to index the posts. Requirements: include the category field in the search results; ensure that users can search for words in the category field; ensure that users can perform drill down filtering based on category. Which index attributes should you configure for the category field?
correctText: B. searchable, facetable, and retrievable
explanation: searchable enables full-text search on the field, facetable enables drill-down filtering (facets), and retrievable returns the field in results.
mlTitle: Create an index in Azure AI Search

question: SIMULATION (hands-on lab, no multiple-choice options): You plan to build an API that will identify whether an image includes a Microsoft Surface Pro or Surface Studio. You need to deploy a service in Azure Cognitive Services for the API. The service must be named AAA12345678 and must be in the East US region. The solution must use the Free pricing tier. To complete this task, sign in to the Azure portal.
correctText: Create a Custom Vision resource named AAA12345678 in East US on the Free (F0) tier (image classification of specific products requires Custom Vision).
explanation: Hands-on simulation: classifying specific products (Surface Pro vs Surface Studio) requires a custom image classifier - create a Custom Vision resource with the specified name, region, and Free tier.

question: SIMULATION (hands-on lab, no multiple-choice options): You need to build an API that uses the Azure Cognitive Services service named AAA12345678 to identify whether an image includes a Microsoft Surface Pro or Surface Studio, using the sample images in the C:\Resources\Images folder. To complete this task, sign in to the Azure portal.
correctText: In Custom Vision, create a classification project bound to AAA12345678, create Surface Pro and Surface Studio tags, upload and tag the sample images, then train and publish the model.
explanation: Hands-on simulation: build and train a Custom Vision image classification project, tagging the sample images by product, then publish for prediction.
mlTitle: Build a classifier with Custom Vision

question: SIMULATION (hands-on lab, no multiple-choice options): You need to get insights from a video file located in C:\Resources\Video\Media.mp4. Save the insights to C:\Resources\Video\Insights.json. To complete this task, sign in to the Azure Video Analyzer for Media at https://www.videoindexer.ai/ using admin@abc.com.
correctText: Upload Media.mp4 to Azure Video Indexer (videoindexer.ai), let it process, then download/export the insights JSON and save it as Insights.json.
explanation: Hands-on simulation: Azure AI Video Indexer extracts insights from uploaded video; the insights can be downloaded as JSON.

question: SIMULATION (hands-on lab, no multiple-choice options): You plan to analyze stock photography and automatically generate captions for the images. You need to create a service in Azure to analyze the images. The service must be named caption12345678 and must be in the East US region on the Free pricing tier. In C:\Resources\Caption\Params.json, enter the value for Key 1 and the endpoint for the new service. To complete this task, sign in to the Azure portal.
correctText: Create a Computer Vision resource named caption12345678 in East US on the Free (F0) tier, then copy Key 1 and the Endpoint from the resource's Keys and Endpoint page into Params.json.
explanation: Hands-on simulation: image captioning uses Computer Vision; after creating the resource, retrieve Key 1 and the endpoint and record them in the params file.

question: SIMULATION (hands-on lab, no multiple-choice options): You plan to build an application that will use caption12345678. The application will be deployed to a virtual network named VNet1. You need to ensure that only virtual machines on VNet1 can access caption12345678. To complete this task, sign in to the Azure portal.
correctText: In the caption12345678 resource Networking settings, set access to Selected Networks and Private Endpoints and add the VNet1 subnet (configure a virtual network rule / service endpoint), denying all other networks.
explanation: Hands-on simulation: restrict an Azure AI services resource to a specific VNet by configuring the resource's networking (virtual network) rules to allow only VNet1.
mlTitle: Configure Azure AI services virtual networks

question: SIMULATION (hands-on lab, no multiple-choice options): You need to ensure that a user named admin@abc.com can regenerate the subscription keys of AAA12345678. The solution must use the principle of least privilege. To complete this task, sign in to the Azure portal.
correctText: On the AAA12345678 resource Access control (IAM), assign admin@abc.com the 'Cognitive Services Contributor' role (which allows key regeneration) rather than Owner, following least privilege.
explanation: Hands-on simulation: the Cognitive Services Contributor role grants key management (regeneration) without broader Owner rights, satisfying least privilege.
mlTitle: Azure AI services role-based access control

question: You have an Azure IoT hub that receives sensor data from machinery. You need to build an app that will perform the following actions: perform anomaly detection across multiple correlated sensors; identify the root cause of process stops; send incident alerts. The solution must minimize development time. Which Azure service should you use?
correctText: A. Azure Metrics Advisor
explanation: Metrics Advisor provides multivariate anomaly detection across correlated metrics, root cause analysis, and built-in alerting, minimizing development effort.

question: You have an app that analyzes images by using the Computer Vision API. You need to configure the app to provide an output for users who are vision impaired. The solution must provide the output in complete sentences. Which API call should you perform?
correctText: D. describeImageInStreamAsync
explanation: Describe Image returns human-readable descriptions (complete sentences/captions) of an image, ideal for vision-impaired users.

question:
You have a Custom Vision service project that performs object detection. The project uses the General domain for classification and contains a trained model. You need to export the model for use on a network that is disconnected from the internet. Which three actions should you perform in sequence?

Actions: Change the classification type; Export the model; Retrain the model; Change Domains to General (compact); Create a new classification model.
order: Change Domains to General
Retrain the model
Export the model
correctText: 1) Change Domains to General (compact); 2) Retrain the model; 3) Export the model.
explanation: Only compact domains can be exported, so you change the domain to General (compact), retrain so the model uses the compact domain, then export the model for offline use.

question: You are building an AI solution that will use Sentiment Analysis results from surveys to calculate bonuses for customer service staff. You need to ensure that the solution meets the Microsoft responsible AI principles. What should you do?
options: A. Add a human review and approval step before making decisions that affect the staff's financial situation.
B. Include the Sentiment Analysis results when surveys return a low confidence score.
C. Use all the surveys, including surveys by customers who requested that their account be deleted and their data be removed.
D. Publish the raw survey data to a central location and provide the staff with access to the location.
correctText: A. Add a human review and approval step before making decisions that affect the staff's financial situation.
explanation: The accountability/human oversight principle requires meaningful human review before AI is used to make consequential decisions, such as those affecting people's finances.

question: You have an Azure subscription that contains a Language service resource named ta1 and a virtual network named vnet1. You need to ensure that only resources in vnet1 can access ta1. What should you configure?
options: A. a network security group (NSG) for vnet1
C. the virtual network settings for ta1
D. a Language service container for ta1
correctText: C. the virtual network settings for ta1
explanation: Configuring the resource's virtual network (networking) settings to allow only vnet1 restricts access to that VNet.
mlTitle: Configure Azure AI services virtual networks

question: You are developing a monitoring system that will analyze engine sensor data, such as rotation speed, angle, temperature, and pressure. The system must generate an alert in response to atypical values. What should you include in the solution?
options: A. Application Insights in Azure Monitor
B. metric alerts in Azure Monitor
correctText: C. Multivariate Anomaly Detection
explanation: Multiple correlated variables (rotation speed, angle, temperature, pressure) require multivariate anomaly detection, which models relationships across variables.

question: You have an app named App1 that uses an Azure Cognitive Services model to identify anomalies in a time series data stream. You need to run App1 in a location that has limited connectivity. The solution must minimize costs. What should you use to host the model?
options: C. a Kubernetes cluster hosted in an Azure Stack Hub integrated system
correctText: D. the Docker Engine
explanation: Running the Anomaly Detector container directly on the Docker Engine is the lowest-cost option for a disconnected/limited-connectivity location, avoiding AKS/ACI/Azure Stack overhead.
mlTitle: Install and run Anomaly Detector containers

question:
You have an Azure Cognitive Search resource named Search1 used by multiple apps. You need to secure Search1: prevent access from the internet; limit the access of each app to specific queries. What should you do?
1. To prevent access from the internet (options: Configure an IP firewall; Create a private endpoint; Use Azure roles).
2. To limit access to queries (options: Create a private endpoint; Use Azure roles; Use key authentication).
correctText: 1) Create a private endpoint; 2) Use Azure roles.
explanation: A private endpoint disables public internet access, and Azure role-based access control limits what each app (identity) can do, enabling per-app query restrictions.
mlTitle: Azure role-based access control in Azure AI Search

question: You are building a solution that will detect anomalies in sensor data from the previous 24 hours. You need to ensure that the solution scans the entire dataset, at the same time, for anomalies. Which type of detection should you use?
correctText: A. batch
explanation: Batch detection analyzes the entire time series at once, which is required when scanning the whole dataset simultaneously.
mlTitle: Anomaly Detector - batch vs streaming detection

question:
You are building an app that will scan confidential documents and use the Language service to analyze the contents. You provision an Azure Cognitive Services resource. You need to ensure the app can make requests to the Language service endpoint while keeping confidential documents on-premises. Which three actions should you perform in sequence?

Actions: Run the container and specify an App ID and Client Secret; Provision an on-premises Kubernetes cluster that is isolated from the internet; Pull an image from the Microsoft Container Registry (MCR); Run the container and specify an API key and the Endpoint URL of the Cognitive Services resource; Provision an on-premises Kubernetes cluster that has internet connectivity; Pull an image from Docker Hub; Provision an Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) resource.
order: Provision an on-premises Kubernetes cluster that is isolated from the internet
Pull an image from the Microsoft Container Registry
Run the container and specify an API key and the Endpoint URL of the Cognitive Services resource
correctText: 1) Provision an on-premises Kubernetes cluster that is isolated from the internet; 2) Pull an image from the Microsoft Container Registry (MCR); 3) Run the container and specify an API key and the Endpoint URL of the Cognitive Services resource.
explanation: Keeping documents on-premises means hosting the Language container on an isolated on-prem Kubernetes cluster; you pull the image from MCR and run it with the API key and billing endpoint of the provisioned Cognitive Services resource.

question:
You have an Azure subscription with Subscription ID 8d3591aa-96b8-4737-ad09-00f9b1ed35ad and Tenant ID 3edfe572-cb54-3ced-ae12-c5c177f39a12. You plan to create a resource that will perform sentiment analysis and OCR using a single key and endpoint. Complete the HTTP request:
https://management.azure.com/[SEGMENT1]/resourceGroups/OCRProject/providers/[PROVIDER]/accounts/CS1?api-version=2021-10-01
Segment1 options: subscriptions/3edfe572-cb54-3ced-ae12-c5c177f39a12, subscriptions/8d3591aa-96b8-4737-ad09-00f9b1ed35ad, tenant/3edfe572-cb54-3ced-ae12-c5c177f39a12, tenant/8d3591aa-96b8-4737-ad09-00f9b1ed35ad. Provider options: Microsoft.ApiManagement, Microsoft.CognitiveServices, Microsoft.ContainerService, Microsoft.KeyVault.
correctText: Segment1 = subscriptions/8d3591aa-96b8-4737-ad09-00f9b1ed35ad; Provider = Microsoft.CognitiveServices
explanation: ARM resource URLs use the subscription ID (not tenant ID), and a single key/endpoint multi-service resource is created under the Microsoft.CognitiveServices provider.
mlTitle: Create a multi-service Azure AI services resource

question: You have an Azure subscription that contains an Anomaly Detector resource. You deploy a Docker host server named Server1 to the on-premises network. You need to host an instance of the Anomaly Detector service on Server1. Which parameter should you include in the docker run command?
correctText: B. Billing
explanation: Azure AI containers require the Billing parameter (along with ApiKey and Eula=accept) so the container can report usage to the associated Azure resource.

question: You are building an app that will use the Speech service. You need to ensure that the app can authenticate to the service by using a Microsoft Azure Active Directory (Azure AD), part of Microsoft Entra, token. Which two actions should you perform? (Choose two.)
options: A. Enable a virtual network service endpoint.
E. Create a Conditional Access policy.
correctText: B. Configure a custom subdomain and D. Create a private endpoint.
explanation: Azure AD token authentication for Cognitive Services requires a custom subdomain (regional endpoints don't support AAD tokens); the marked second answer is creating a private endpoint. The custom subdomain (B) is the well-documented hard requirement.
mlTitle: Authenticate requests to Azure AI services

correctText: Field = "capacity"; Value = 100
explanation: Azure OpenAI deployment SKU uses 'capacity' in units of 1,000 tokens-per-minute. With the standard ~6 requests-per-minute per 1,000 TPM ratio, 600 RPM requires a capacity of 100.

question:
You have an app that manages feedback. You need to ensure that the app can detect negative comments by using the Sentiment Analysis API in Azure AI Language. The solution must ensure that the managed feedback remains on your company's internal network. Which three actions should you perform in sequence? (More than one order may be correct.)

Actions: Identify the Language service endpoint URL and query the prediction endpoint; Provision the Language service resource in Azure; Run the container and query the prediction endpoint; Deploy a Docker container to an on-premises server; Deploy a Docker container to an Azure container instance.
order: Provision the Language service resource in Azure
Deploy a Docker container to an on-premises server
Run the container and query the prediction endpoint
correctText: 1) Provision the Language service resource in Azure; 2) Deploy a Docker container to an on-premises server; 3) Run the container and query the prediction endpoint.
explanation: To keep feedback on the internal network, run the Sentiment Analysis container on-premises: first provision the Language resource (for billing/key), deploy the Docker container to an on-premises server, then run it and query the local prediction endpoint.

question:
You have an Azure OpenAI resource named AI1 that hosts three deployments of the GPT 3.5 model, each optimized for a unique workload. You plan to deploy three apps that each access AI1 via the REST API and use the deployment optimized for the app's workload. You need to provide each app with access to AI1 and the appropriate deployment, ensuring only the apps can access AI1. What should you use?
1. Provide access to AI1 by using (options: An API key; A bearer token; A shared access signature (SAS) token).
2. Connect to the deployment by using (options: An API key; A deployment endpoint; A deployment name; A deployment type).
correctText: 1) An API key; 2) A deployment name
explanation: An API key restricts access to AI1 to holders of the key, and in the Azure OpenAI REST API each app selects its specific model deployment by the deployment name in the request path.

question: You build a bot by using the Microsoft Bot Framework SDK. You start the bot on a local computer. You need to validate the functionality of the bot. What should you do before you connect to the bot?
options: A. Run the Bot Framework Emulator.
B. Run the Bot Framework Composer.
C. Register the bot with Azure Bot Service.
correctText: A. Run the Bot Framework Emulator.
explanation: The Bot Framework Emulator is a desktop tool used to test and debug bots running locally before connecting to them.
mlTitle: Test and debug bots with the Bot Framework Emulator

question: You have an Azure OpenAI model named AI1. You are building a web app named App1 by using the Azure OpenAI SDK. You need to configure App1 to connect to AI1. What information must you provide?
options: A. the endpoint, key, and model name
B. the deployment name, key, and model name
C. the deployment name, endpoint, and key
D. the endpoint, key, and model type
correctText: C. the deployment name, endpoint, and key
explanation: The Azure OpenAI SDK connects using the resource endpoint, an API key, and the deployment name (not the underlying model name) that identifies the deployed model.

question: You are building a solution in Azure that will use Azure Cognitive Service for Language to process sensitive customer data. You need to ensure that only specific Azure processes can access the Language service. The solution must minimize administrative effort. What should you include in the solution?
correctText: D. virtual network rules
explanation: Virtual network rules (service endpoints) restrict the Language service to specific subnets/resources with minimal administrative effort.
mlTitle: Configure Azure AI services virtual networks

question: You plan to perform predictive maintenance. You collect IoT sensor data from 100 industrial machines for a year. Each machine has 60 different sensors that generate data at one-minute intervals. In total, you have 5,000 time series datasets. You need to identify unusual values in each time series to help predict machinery failures. Which Azure service should you use?
correctText: D. Azure AI Anomaly Detector
explanation: Anomaly Detector identifies unusual values (anomalies) in time series data, which is what predictive maintenance on sensor data requires.

question:
You plan to deploy a containerized Azure Cognitive Services service for sentiment analysis. You configure https://contoso.cognitiveservices.azure.com as the endpoint URI. You need to run the container on an Azure VM by using Docker. Complete the command:
docker run --rm -it -p 5000:5000 --memory 8g --cpus 1 \
[IMAGE] \
Eula=accept \
Billing=[BILLING] \
ApiKey=xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
Image options: http://contoso.blob.core.windows.net, https://contoso.cognitiveservices.azure.com, mcr.microsoft.com/azure-cognitive-services/textanalytics/keyphrase, mcr.microsoft.com/azure-cognitive-services/textanalytics/sentiment. Billing options: same four.
correctText: Image = mcr.microsoft.com/azure-cognitive-services/textanalytics/sentiment; Billing = https://contoso.cognitiveservices.azure.com
explanation: Run the sentiment container image from MCR and set the Billing parameter to the Cognitive Services endpoint URI.

question: You are developing a system that will monitor temperature data from a data stream. The system must generate an alert in response to atypical values. The solution must minimize development effort. What should you include in the solution?
options: C. metric alerts in Azure Monitor
correctText: D. Univariate Anomaly Detection
explanation: A single variable (temperature) over time is a univariate series, so Univariate Anomaly Detection is the right, low-effort choice.

question: You have a Microsoft OneDrive folder that contains a 20-GB video file named File1.avi. You need to index File1.avi by using the Azure Video Indexer website. What should you do?
options: A. Upload File1.avi to www.youtube.com, and then copy the URL of the video to the Azure AI Video Indexer website.
B. Download File1.avi to a local computer, and then upload the file to the Azure AI Video Indexer website.
C. From OneDrive, create a download link, and then copy the link to the Azure AI Video Indexer website.
D. From OneDrive, create a sharing link for File1.avi, and then copy the link to the Azure AI Video Indexer website.
correctText: D. From OneDrive, create a sharing link for File1.avi, and then copy the link to the Azure AI Video Indexer website.
explanation: Video Indexer can index a video from a URL; creating a OneDrive sharing link lets Video Indexer access the large file without uploading it directly (which is limited in size).
mlTitle: Upload and index videos with Azure AI Video Indexer

question: You have an Azure subscription that contains an Azure AI Service resource named CSAccount1 and a virtual network named VNet1. CSAccount1 is connected to VNet1. You need to ensure that only specific resources can access CSAccount1. Requirements: prevent external access to CSAccount1; minimize administrative effort. Which two actions should you perform? (Choose two.)
options: A. In VNet1, enable a service endpoint for CSAccount1.
B. In CSAccount1, configure the Access control (IAM) settings.
C. In VNet1, modify the virtual network settings.
D. In VNet1, create a virtual subnet.
E. In CSAccount1, modify the virtual network settings.
correctText: A. In VNet1, enable a service endpoint for CSAccount1 and E. In CSAccount1, modify the virtual network settings.
explanation: Enable the Cognitive Services service endpoint on the VNet1 subnet, then in CSAccount1's networking (virtual network) settings allow only VNet1, blocking external access with minimal effort.
mlTitle: Configure Azure AI services virtual networks

question: You are building an internet-based training solution. The solution requires that a user's camera and microphone remain enabled. You need to monitor a video stream of the user and detect when the user asks an instructor a question. The solution must minimize development effort. What should you include in the solution?
options: A. speech-to-text in the Azure AI Speech service
B. language detection in Azure AI Language Service
C. the Face service in Azure AI Vision
D. object detection in Azure AI Custom Vision
correctText: A. speech-to-text in the Azure AI Speech service
explanation: Detecting when the user asks a spoken question is done by transcribing the audio with speech-to-text in the Azure AI Speech service.

question: You have an Azure DevOps pipeline named Pipeline1 that is used to deploy an app. Pipeline1 includes a step that will create an Azure AI services account. You need to add a step to Pipeline1 that will identify the created Azure AI services account. The solution must minimize development effort. Which Azure Command-Line Interface (CLI) command should you run?
correctText: C. az cognitiveservices account show
explanation: The az cognitiveservices account show command returns the properties (including resource ID) of a specific Azure AI services account, identifying the account created by the pipeline.

question: You have 1,000 scanned images of hand-written survey responses. The surveys do NOT have a consistent layout. You have an Azure subscription that contains an Azure AI Document Intelligence resource named AIdoc1. You open Document Intelligence Studio and create a new project. You need to extract data from the survey responses. The solution must minimize development effort. To where should you upload the images, and which type of model should you use? Upload to: [An Azure Cosmos DB account / An Azure Files share / An Azure Storage account]; Model type: [Custom neural / Custom template / Identity document (ID)].
correctText: Upload to: An Azure Storage account; Model type: Custom neural.
explanation: Document Intelligence Studio reads training/source files from an Azure Storage (blob) account, and because the surveys have no consistent layout you must use a custom neural model, which handles structured, semi-structured, and handwritten forms.

question: You are developing an application that will use the Computer Vision client library. The application has the following code. (public async Task AnalyzeImage(ComputerVisionClient client, string localImage) { List<VisualFeatureTypes> features = new List<VisualFeatureTypes>() { VisualFeatureTypes.Description, VisualFeatureTypes.Tags }; using (Stream imageStream = File.OpenRead(localImage)) { try { ImageAnalysis results = await client.AnalyzeImageInStreamAsync(imageStream, features); foreach (var caption in results.Description.Captions) { Console.WriteLine($"{caption.Text} with confidence {caption.Confidence}"); } foreach (var tag in results.Tags) { Console.WriteLine($"{tag.Name} {tag.Confidence}"); } } catch (Exception ex) { Console.WriteLine(ex.Message); } } }) Statement: The code will perform face recognition.
correctText: No. The requested visual features are Description and Tags only; Face is not requested, so the code does not perform face recognition. (The other two statements in this hotspot - 'will list tags and their associated confidence' and 'will read a file from the local file system' - are both Yes.)
explanation: Only Description and Tags features are requested and AnalyzeImageInStreamAsync reads a local stream; face recognition would require the Faces visual feature, which is not included.

question: You are developing a method that uses the Computer Vision client library. The method will perform optical character recognition (OCR) in images. The method has the following code (C#): it calls client.ReadAsync, then GetReadResultAsync(Guid.Parse(operationId)) and iterates results.AnalyzeResult.ReadResults. During testing, you discover that the call to the GetReadResultAsync method occurs before the read operation is complete. You need to prevent the GetReadResultAsync method from proceeding until the read operation is complete. Which two actions should you perform? Each correct answer presents part of the solution.
options: B. Add code to verify the results.Status value.
C. Add code to verify the status of the txtHeaders.Status value.
D. Wrap the call to GetReadResultAsync within a loop that contains a delay.
correctText: B. Add code to verify the results.Status value; and D. Wrap the call to GetReadResultAsync within a loop that contains a delay.
explanation: The Read API is asynchronous, so you must poll: loop with a delay and check results.Status until it is 'succeeded' before processing the read results.

question: You have a Computer Vision resource named contoso1 that is hosted in the West US Azure region. You need to use contoso1 to make a different size of a product photo by using the smart cropping feature. How should you complete the API URL? curl -H "Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key: xxx" -o "sample.png" -H "Content-Type: application/json" "[https://api.projectoxford.ai / https://contoso1.cognitiveservices.azure.com / https://westus.api.cognitive.microsoft.com]/vision/v3.1/[areaOfInterest / detect / generateThumbnail]?width=100&height=100&smartCropping=true" -d "{\"url\":\"https://upload.litwareinc.org/litware/bicycle.jpg\"}"
correctText: Host: https://contoso1.cognitiveservices.azure.com ; Operation: generateThumbnail. Full URL: https://contoso1.cognitiveservices.azure.com/vision/v3.1/generateThumbnail?width=100&height=100&smartCropping=true
explanation: Calls use the resource's custom subdomain endpoint (contoso1.cognitiveservices.azure.com), and smart cropping to resize an image uses the generateThumbnail operation with smartCropping=true.

question: You are developing a webpage that will use the Azure Video Analyzer for Media (previously Video Indexer) service to display videos of internal company meetings. You embed the Player widget and the Cognitive Insights widget into the page. You need to configure the widgets to meet the following requirements: Ensure that users can search for keywords; Display the names and faces of people in the video; Show captions in the video in English (United States). How should you complete the URL for each widget? Cognitive Insights Widget: https://www.videoindexer.ai/embed/insights/<accountId>/<videoId>/?widgets=[value]&controls=[value]. Player Widget: https://www.videoindexer.ai/embed/player/<accountId>/<videoId>/?showCaptions=[value]&captions=[value]. Values: en-US, false, people,keywords, people,search, search, true.
correctText: Cognitive Insights Widget: widgets=people,keywords and controls=search. Player Widget: showCaptions=true and captions=en-US.
explanation: The insights widget uses widgets=people,keywords (faces/names plus keyword insights) with controls=search to enable keyword search, and the player widget uses showCaptions=true with captions=en-US for English (US) captions.

question: You train a Custom Vision model to identify a company's products by using the Retail domain. You plan to deploy the model as part of an app for Android phones. You need to prepare the model for deployment. Which three actions should you perform in sequence? Actions: Change the model domain; Retrain the model; Test the model; Export the model.
order: Change the model domain (to a compact domain, e.g. Retail (compact))
Retrain the model
Export the model
correctText: 1. Change the model domain (to a compact domain, e.g. Retail (compact)); 2. Retrain the model; 3. Export the model.
explanation: Exporting a Custom Vision model for mobile requires a compact domain, so you change the domain, retrain, then export the model (e.g., to TensorFlow for Android).

question: You are developing an application to recognize employees' faces by using the Face Recognition API. Images of the faces will be accessible from a URI endpoint. The application has add_face which POSTs to /face/v1.0/persongroups/{person_group_id}/persons/{person_id}/persistedFaces with body {'url': image_uri}. Statement: The code will add a face image to a person object in a person group.
correctText: Yes. The POST to persongroups/{id}/persons/{id}/persistedFaces adds a face to a person in a person group. (The other two statements - 'will work for up to 10,000 people' and 'add_face can be called multiple times to add multiple face images to a person object' - are both Yes as well.)
explanation: The call adds a persisted face to a person within a person group; a person group supports up to 10,000 persons and add_face can be invoked repeatedly to add multiple faces per person.

question: You have a Custom Vision resource named acvdev in a development environment. You have a Custom Vision resource named acvprod in a production environment. In acvdev, you build an object detection model named obj1 in a project named proj1. You need to move obj1 to acvprod. Which three actions should you perform in sequence? Actions: Use the Export Project endpoint on acvdev; Use the Get Projects endpoint on acvdev; Use the Export Iteration endpoint on acvdev; Use the Get Iterations endpoint on acvdev.
order: Per the marked items:
Use the Get Projects endpoint on acvdev
Use the Export Project endpoint on acvdev
Use the Export Iteration endpoint on acvdev
correctText: Per the marked items: 1. Use the Get Projects endpoint on acvdev; 2. Use the Export Project endpoint on acvdev; 3. Use the Export Iteration endpoint on acvdev.
explanation: To move a project between Custom Vision resources you call GetProjects to find proj1, then ExportProject on acvdev to obtain an export token used to import it into acvprod.
mlTitle: Copy and move Custom Vision projects

question: You are developing an application that will recognize faults in components produced on a factory production line. The components are specific to your business. You need to use the Custom Vision API to help detect common faults. Which three actions should you perform in sequence? Actions: Train the classifier model; Upload and tag images; Initialize the training dataset; Train the object detection model; Create a project.
order: Create a project
Upload and tag images
Train the classifier model
correctText: 1. Create a project; 2. Upload and tag images; 3. Train the classifier model.
explanation: The standard Custom Vision classification workflow is to create a project, upload and tag training images, then train the classifier model.

question: You are building a model that will be used in an iOS app. You have images of cats and dogs. Each image contains either a cat or a dog. You need to use the Custom Vision service to detect whether the image is of a cat or a dog. How should you configure the project in the Custom Vision portal? Project Types: [Classification / Object Detection]; Classification Types: [Multiclass (Single tag per image) / Multilabel (Multiple tags per image)]; Domains: [Audit / Food / General / General (compact) / Landmarks / Landmarks (compact) / Retail / Retail (compact)].
correctText: Project Types: Classification; Classification Types: Multiclass (Single tag per image); Domains: General (compact).
explanation: Each image has exactly one label so use Classification with Multiclass (single tag), and deployment to an iOS app requires an exportable General (compact) domain.

question: You have an Azure Video Analyzer for Media (previously Video Indexer) service that is used to provide a search interface over company videos on your company's website. You need to be able to search for videos based on who is present in the video. What should you do?
options: A. Create a person model and associate the model to the videos.
B. Create person objects and provide face images for each object.
C. Invite the entire staff of the company to Video Indexer.
D. Edit the faces in the videos.
E. Upload names to a language model.
correctText: A. Create a person model and associate the model to the videos.
explanation: In Video Indexer you build a custom person model (with named faces) and associate it with your videos so faces are recognized and searchable.
mlTitle: Customize a person model in Video Indexer

question: You use the Custom Vision service to build a classifier. After training is complete, you need to evaluate the classifier. Which two metrics are available for review? Each correct answer presents a complete solution.
correctText: A. recall; and D. precision.
explanation: Custom Vision reports Precision, Recall, and mAP for a trained classifier; precision and recall are the available metrics among the listed options.
mlTitle: Evaluate and improve a Custom Vision classifier

question: You are developing a call to the Face API. The call must find similar faces from an existing list named employeefaces. The employeefaces list contains 60,000 images. How should you complete the body of the HTTP request? Body: { "faceId": "18c51a87-3a69-47a8-aedc-a54745f708a1", [value]: "employeefaces", "maxNumOfCandidatesReturned": 1, "mode": [value] }. Values: "faceListId", "LargeFaceListId", "matchFace", "matchPerson".
correctText: First box: "LargeFaceListId"; mode box: "matchFace".
explanation: A standard face list holds up to 1,000 faces, so 60,000 images require a large face list (LargeFaceListId), and FindSimilar with mode matchFace returns visually similar faces.

question: You are developing a photo application that will find photos of a person based on a sample image by using the Face API. You need to create a POST request to find the photos. How should you complete the request? POST {Endpoint}/face/v1.0/[value]. Request Body: { "faceId": "c5c24a82-6845-4031-9d5d-978df9175426", "largeFaceListId": "sample_list", "maxNumOfCandidatesReturned": 10, "mode": [value] }. Values: detect, findsimilar, group, identify, matchFace, matchPerson, verify.
correctText: URL operation: findsimilar; mode: matchPerson.
explanation: Finding photos of the same person uses the FindSimilar operation with mode=matchPerson, which returns faces likely belonging to the same individual.

question: You develop a test method to verify the results retrieved from a call to the Computer Vision API. The call analyzes the existence of company logos in images and returns a collection of brands named brands. Code (Python): for brand in image_analysis.brands: if brand.confidence >= 0.75: print(f"Logo of {brand.name} between {brand.rectangle.x}, {brand.rectangle.y} and {brand.rectangle.w}, {brand.rectangle.h}"). Statement: The code will return the name of each detected brand with a confidence equal to or higher than 75 percent.
correctText: Yes. The code prints brand names where confidence >= 0.75 and the top-left corner (x,y). (Statement 2 'top-left corner' = Yes; statement 3 'bottom-right corner' = No, because w/h are width/height, not bottom-right coordinates.)
explanation: The filter brand.confidence >= 0.75 returns names at >=75 percent confidence; rectangle.x/y give the top-left corner, while w/h are width and height (not bottom-right coordinates), so the bottom-right statement is No.

question: You develop an application that uses the Face API. You need to add multiple images to a person group. How should you complete the code? Parallel.For(0, PersonCount, async i => { Guid personId = persons[i].PersonId; string personImageDir = $"/path/to/person/{i}/images"; foreach (string imagePath in Directory.GetFiles(personImageDir, "*.jpg")) { using ([dropdown1] t = File.OpenRead(imagePath)) { await faceClient.PersonGroupPerson.[dropdown2](personGroupId, personId, t); } } }); dropdown1: [Stream / ...]; dropdown2: [AddFaceFromStreamAsync / AddFaceFromUrlAsync / CreateAsync / GetAsync].
correctText: dropdown1: Stream; dropdown2: AddFaceFromStreamAsync.
explanation: File.OpenRead returns a Stream, so the variable is typed Stream and you call AddFaceFromStreamAsync to upload each face image from the local stream.
mlTitle: Add faces to a person group

question: Your company uses an Azure Cognitive Services solution to detect faces in uploaded images. The detect method uses requestParameters = "detectionModel=detection_01&returnFaceId=true&returnFaceLandmarks=false" and posts to endpoint + "/face/v1.0/detect". You discover that the solution frequently fails to detect faces in blurred images and in images that contain sideways faces. You need to increase the likelihood that the solution can detect faces in blurred images and images that contain sideways faces. What should you do?
options: A. Use a different version of the Face API.
B. Use the Computer Vision service instead of the Face service.
C. Use the Identify method instead of the Detect method.
correctText: D. Change the detection model.
explanation: Switching from detection_01 to detection_02 (or detection_03) improves detection of small, blurred, side-view, and rotated faces.

question: You have the following Python function for creating Azure Cognitive Services resources programmatically: def create_resource(resource_name, kind, account_tier, location): parameters = CognitiveServicesAccount(sku=Sku(name=account_tier), kind=kind, location=location, properties={}); result = client.accounts.create(resource_group_name, resource_name, parameters). You need to call the function to create a free Azure resource in the West US Azure region. The resource will be used to generate captions of images automatically. Which code should you use?
correctText: A. create_resource("res1", "ComputerVision", "F0", "westus")
explanation: Generating image captions uses the Computer Vision (ComputerVision) kind, F0 is the free pricing tier, and westus is the requested region.

question: You are developing a method that uses the Computer Vision client library. The method will perform optical character recognition (OCR) in images. Code (Python): read_response = computervision_client.read(url_file, raw=True); read_operation_location = read_response.headers["Operation-Location"]; operation_id = read_operation_location.split("/")[-1]; read_result = computervision_client.get_read_result(operation_id); ... During testing, you discover that the call to get_read_result occurs before the read operation is complete. You need to prevent it from proceeding until the read operation is complete. Which two actions should you perform?
options: B. Add code to verify the read_results.status value.
C. Add code to verify the status of the read_operation_location value.
D. Wrap the call to get_read_result within a loop that contains a delay.
correctText: B. Add code to verify the read_results.status value; and D. Wrap the call to get_read_result within a loop that contains a delay.
explanation: The Read API is asynchronous, so you poll in a loop with a delay and check read_results.status until it is 'succeeded' before reading the results.

question: You are building an app that will enable users to upload images. The solution must meet the following requirements: Automatically suggest alt text for the images; Detect inappropriate images and block them; Minimize development effort. You need to recommend a computer vision endpoint for each requirement. Options for each dropdown: contentmoderator/moderate/v1.0/ProcessImage/Evaluate; customvision/v3.1/prediction/projectId/classify/iterations/publishedName/image; vision/v3.2/analyze/?visualFeatures=Adult,Description; vision/v3.2/describe?maxCandidates=1.
correctText: Generate alt text: https://westus.api.cognitive.microsoft.com/vision/v3.2/analyze/?visualFeatures=Adult,Description ; Detect inappropriate content: https://westus.api.cognitive.microsoft.com/vision/v3.2/analyze/?visualFeatures=Adult,Description
explanation: A single Image Analysis analyze call with visualFeatures=Adult,Description returns a description (alt text) and adult/racy scores for blocking inappropriate images, minimizing development effort.

question: You need to build a solution that will use optical character recognition (OCR) to scan sensitive documents by using the Computer Vision API. The solution must NOT be deployed to the public cloud. What should you do?
options: A. Build an on-premises web app to query the Computer Vision endpoint.
B. Host the Computer Vision endpoint in a container on an on-premises server.
C. Host an exported Open Neural Network Exchange (ONNX) model on an on-premises server.
D. Build an Azure web app to query the Computer Vision endpoint.
correctText: B. Host the Computer Vision endpoint in a container on an on-premises server.
explanation: Azure AI Vision Read OCR is available as a Docker container that can run on-premises, keeping sensitive documents out of the public cloud.

question: You have an Azure Cognitive Search solution and a collection of handwritten letters stored as JPEG files. You plan to index the collection. The solution must ensure that queries can be performed on the contents of the letters. You need to create an indexer that has a skillset. Which skill should you include?
correctText: B. optical character recognition (OCR).
explanation: The OCR cognitive skill extracts printed and handwritten text from images (JPEGs), making the letter contents searchable in the index.

question: You have a library that contains thousands of images. You need to tag the images as photographs, drawings, or clipart. Which service endpoint and response property should you use? (NOTE: the answer-area exhibit did not render in the source PDF, so the marked selection could not be read.)
correctText: Documented answer: Service endpoint = Image Analysis (vision analyze with visualFeatures=ImageType); Response property = imageType (returns clipArtType and lineDrawingType). (Exhibit missing in PDF; based on Azure documentation.)
explanation: The Image Analysis ImageType visual feature returns an imageType object whose clipArtType and lineDrawingType values classify an image as clip art, a line drawing, or a photograph.

question: You have an app that captures live video of exam candidates. You need to use the Face service to validate that the subjects of the videos are real people. What should you do?
options: A. Call the face detection API and retrieve the face rectangle by using the FaceRectangle attribute.
B. Call the face detection API repeatedly and check for changes to the FaceAttributes.HeadPose attribute.
C. Call the face detection API and use the FaceLandmarks attribute to calculate the distance between pupils.
D. Call the face detection API repeatedly and check for changes to the FaceAttributes.Accessories attribute.
correctText: B. Call the face detection API repeatedly and check for changes to the FaceAttributes.HeadPose attribute.
explanation: Repeatedly checking the HeadPose attribute for movement (a simple liveness check) helps confirm a live person rather than a static photo.

question: You make an API request and receive the results shown in the following exhibits. Use the drop-down menus to select the answer choice that completes each statement based on the information presented in the graphic. (NOTE: both exhibit images and the dropdown statements did not render in the source PDF, so the question content and marked answers could not be determined.)
correctText: Unable to determine - the exhibit graphics and dropdown statements are missing/broken in the source PDF.
explanation: The required exhibit images did not render in the PDF, so the specific statements and correct selections cannot be transcribed.

question: You have an Azure subscription that contains an AI enrichment pipeline in Azure Cognitive Search and an Azure Storage account that has 10 GB of scanned documents and images. You need to index the documents and images in the storage account. The solution must minimize how long it takes to build the index. What should you do?
options: A. From the Azure portal, configure parallel indexing.
B. From the Azure portal, configure scheduled indexing.
C. Configure field mappings by using the REST API.
D. Create a text-based indexer by using the REST API.
correctText: A. From the Azure portal, configure parallel indexing.
explanation: Parallel indexing (multiple partitions processing documents concurrently) reduces the overall time to build an index over large or compute-heavy enrichment workloads.
mlTitle: Index large data sets in Azure AI Search

question: You need to analyze video content to identify any mentions of specific company names. Which three actions should you perform in sequence? Actions: Add the specific company names to the exclude list; Add the specific company names to the include list; From Content model customization, select Language; Sign in to the Custom Vision website; Sign in to the Azure Video Analyzer for Media website; From Content model customization, select Brands.
order: Sign in to the Azure Video Analyzer for Media website
From Content model customization, select Brands
correctText: 1. Sign in to the Azure Video Analyzer for Media website; 2. From Content model customization, select Brands; 3. Add the specific company names to the include list. NOTE: the red mark in the PDF aligned step 2 with 'select Language', but per Azure documentation company/brand detection uses the Brands content model (not Language), so the documented Brands answer is used here.
explanation: Detecting brand/company mentions uses the Video Indexer Brands content model: sign in, open Content model customization and select Brands, then add the company names to the include list.
mlTitle: Customize a Brands model in Video Indexer

question: You have a mobile app that manages printed forms. You need the app to send images of the forms directly to Forms Recognizer to extract relevant information. For compliance reasons, the image files must not be stored in the cloud. In which format should you send the images to the Form Recognizer API endpoint?
correctText: A. raw image binary.
explanation: Sending the image as raw binary (Content-Type: application/octet-stream) lets Form Recognizer analyze the file directly without first storing or referencing it via a cloud URL.

question: You plan to build an app that will generate a list of tags for uploaded images. The app must meet the following requirements: Generate tags in a user's preferred language; Support English, French, and Spanish; Minimize development effort. You need to build a function that will generate the tags for the app. Which Azure service endpoint should you use?
correctText: C. Computer Vision Image Analysis.
explanation: Computer Vision Image Analysis tagging supports returning tags in multiple languages (including English, French, and Spanish) via the language parameter, minimizing development effort.

question: You develop a test method to verify the results retrieved from a call to the Computer Vision API. The call analyzes the existence of company logos in images and returns a collection of brands named brands. Code (C#): foreach (var brand in brands) { if (brand.Confidence >= .75) Console.WriteLine($"Logo of {brand.Name} between {brand.Rectangle.X}, {brand.Rectangle.Y} and {brand.Rectangle.W}, {brand.Rectangle.H}"); }. Statement: The code will display the name of each detected brand with a confidence equal to or higher than 75 percent.
correctText: Yes. The code prints brand names where Confidence >= .75 and the top-left corner (X,Y). (Statement 2 'top-left corner' = Yes; statement 3 'bottom-right corner' = No, since W/H are width/height, not bottom-right coordinates.)
explanation: The filter Confidence >= .75 returns names at >=75 percent confidence; Rectangle.X/Y give the top-left corner, while W/H are width and height (not bottom-right coordinates), so the bottom-right statement is No.

question: You have a factory that produces cardboard packaging for food products. The factory has intermittent internet connectivity. The packages are required to include four samples of each product. You need to build a Custom Vision model that will identify defects in packaging and provide the location of the defects to an operator. The model must ensure that each package contains the four products. Which project type and domain should you use? Options: Food; General; General (compact); Image classification; Logo; Object detection. Targets: Project type: [box]; Domain: [box].
correctText: Project type: Object detection; Domain: General (compact).
explanation: Locating defects and counting four products requires Object detection, and intermittent connectivity means the model must run at the edge, so an exportable General (compact) domain is used.

question: You are building a model to detect objects in images. The performance of the model based on training data is shown in the exhibit: Precision = 100.0%, Recall = 25.0%, mAP = 77.2% (General domain, Iteration 1). Use the drop-down menus to select the answer choice that completes each statement. Statement 1: The percentage of false positives is [0 / 25 / 30 / 50 / 100]. Statement 2: The value for the number of true positives divided by the total number of true positives and false negatives is [0 / 25 / 30 / 50 / 100].
correctText: Statement 1 (percentage of false positives): 0; Statement 2 (TP / (TP + FN), i.e. recall): 25.
explanation: Precision of 100% means there are no false positives (0%); the formula TP/(TP+FN) is recall, which is 25%.
mlTitle: Precision, recall, and mAP in Custom Vision

question: You are building an app that will include one million scanned magazine articles. Each article will be stored as an image file. You need to configure the app to extract text from the images. The solution must minimize development effort. What should you include in the solution?
options: B. the Read API in Computer Vision
D. Azure Cognitive Service for Language
correctText: B. the Read API in Computer Vision.
explanation: The Read API (OCR) is optimized for extracting large amounts of printed and handwritten text from images and documents, ideal for text-heavy scanned magazine articles.
mlTitle: OCR - Read API in Computer Vision

question: You have a 20-GB video file named File1.avi that is stored on a local drive. You need to index File1.avi by using the Azure Video Indexer website. What should you do first?
options: A. Upload File1.avi to an Azure Storage queue.
B. Upload File1.avi to the Azure Video Indexer website.
C. Upload File1.avi to Microsoft OneDrive.
D. Upload File1.avi to the www.youtube.com webpage.
correctText: B. Upload File1.avi to the Azure Video Indexer website.
explanation: The Video Indexer website lets you upload a local file directly (within its size/duration limits), after which it is indexed; no intermediate storage/OneDrive/YouTube step is required.
mlTitle: Upload and index videos with Video Indexer

question: You are building an app that will share user images. You need to configure the app to meet the following requirements: Uploaded images must be scanned and any text must be extracted from the images; Extracted text must be analyzed for the presence of profane language; The solution must minimize development effort. What should you use for each requirement? Text extraction: [Azure AI Language / Azure AI Computer Vision / Content Moderator / Azure AI Custom Vision / Azure AI Document Intelligence]; Profane language detection: [Azure AI Language / Azure AI Computer Vision / Content Moderator / Azure AI Custom Vision / Azure AI Document Intelligence].
correctText: Text extraction: Azure AI Computer Vision; Profane language detection: Content Moderator.
explanation: Azure AI Computer Vision (Read/OCR) extracts text from images, and Content Moderator's text moderation detects profanity in the extracted text.

question: You are building an app that will share user images. You need to configure the app to perform the following actions when a user uploads an image: Categorize the image as either a photograph or a drawing; Generate a caption for the image. The solution must minimize development effort. Which two services should you include in the solution? Each correct answer presents part of the solution. NOTE: Each correct selection is worth one point.
options: A. object detection in Azure AI Computer Vision
B. content tags in Azure AI Computer Vision
C. image descriptions in Azure AI Computer Vision
D. image type detection in Azure AI Computer Vision
E. image classification in Azure AI Custom Vision
correctText: C. image descriptions in Azure AI Computer Vision; D. image type detection in Azure AI Computer Vision
