explanation: Image descriptions generate a human-readable caption, and image type detection classifies an image as clip art/line drawing vs photo, both built into Azure AI Computer Vision with minimal effort.
mlTitle: Computer Vision image descriptions & image type

question: You are building an app that will use the Azure AI Video Indexer service. You plan to train a language model to recognize industry-specific terms. You need to upload a file that contains the industry-specific terms. Which file format should you use?
correctText: B. TXT
explanation: Azure AI Video Indexer language model customization accepts plain text (.txt) files containing the domain-specific terms.

question: DRAG DROP - You have an app that uses Azure AI and a custom trained classifier to identify products in images. You need to add new products to the classifier. The solution must meet the following requirements: Minimize how long it takes to add the products; Minimize development effort. Which five actions should you perform in sequence? Actions: Label the sample images; From Vision Studio, open the project; Publish the model; From the Custom Vision portal, open the project; Retrain the model; Upload sample images of the new products; From the Azure Machine Learning studio, open the workspace.
options: From the Custom Vision portal, open the project.
Upload sample images of the new products.
order: From the Custom Vision portal, open the project
Upload sample images of the new products
Label the sample images
Retrain the model
Publish the model
correctText: 1. From the Custom Vision portal, open the project. 2. Upload sample images of the new products. 3. Label the sample images. 4. Retrain the model. 5. Publish the model.
explanation: In the Custom Vision portal you open the existing project, upload and label the new product images, then retrain and publish the updated model.

question: HOTSPOT - You are developing an application that will use the Azure AI Vision client library. The application has the following code: def analyze_image(local_image): with open(local_image, "rb") as image_stream: image_analysis = client.analyze_image_in_stream(image=image_stream, visual_features=[VisualFeatureTypes.tags, VisualFeatureTypes.description]); for caption in image_analysis.description.captions: print(f"\n{caption.text} with confidence {caption.confidence}"); for tag in image_analysis.tags: print(f"\n{tag.name} with confidence {tag.confidence}"). For each statement, select Yes if true, otherwise No. Statements: (1) The code will perform face recognition. (2) The code will list tags and their associated confidence. (3) The code will read an image file from the local file system.
correctText: (1) The code will perform face recognition = No; (2) The code will list tags and their associated confidence = Yes; (3) The code will read an image file from the local file system = Yes.
explanation: No face feature is requested so no face recognition; the code does iterate image_analysis.tags printing tag.name and tag.confidence, and it opens a local file in binary mode. NOTE: The PDF's red mark labels statement 2 as 'No', but the code clearly lists tags with confidence, so the documented Azure-correct answer is Yes.

question: You are developing a method that uses the Azure AI Vision client library. The method will perform optical character recognition (OCR) in images. The method has the following code: def read_file_url(computervision_client, url_file): read_response = computervision_client.read(url_file, raw=True); read_operation_location = read_response.headers["Operation-Location"]; operation_id = read_operation_location.split("/")[-1]; read_result = computervision_client.get_read_result(operation_id); for page in read_result.analyze_result.read_results: for line in page.lines: print(line.text). During testing, you discover that the call to the get_read_result method occurs before the read operation is complete. You need to prevent the get_read_result method from proceeding until the read operation is complete. Which two actions should you perform? Each correct answer presents part of the solution.
options: B. Add code to verify the read_results.status value.
C. Add code to verify the status of the read_operation_location value.
D. Wrap the call to get_read_result within a loop that contains a delay.
correctText: B. Add code to verify the read_results.status value; D. Wrap the call to get_read_result within a loop that contains a delay.
explanation: The Read API is asynchronous, so you poll get_read_result inside a loop with a delay and check that read_results.status equals 'succeeded' before processing.

question: HOTSPOT - You are developing an app that will use the Azure AI Vision API to analyze an image. You need to configure the request that will be used by the app to identify whether an image is clipart or a line drawing. How should you complete the request? Request template: [HTTP method] https://*.cognitiveservices.azure.com/vision/v3.2/analyze?visualFeatures=[value]&details={string}&language=e. HTTP method options: GET / PATCH / POST. visualFeatures value options: description / imageType / objects / tags.
correctText: HTTP method = POST; visualFeatures = imageType.
explanation: The Analyze Image call uses HTTP POST, and visualFeatures=imageType returns whether the image is clip art or a line drawing.

question: HOTSPOT - You have an Azure subscription that contains an Azure AI Video Indexer account. You need to add a custom brand and logo to the indexer and configure an exclusion for the custom brand. How should you complete the REST API call? JSON body: { "referenceUrl": "https://www.contoso.com/Contoso", "id": 97974, "name": "Contoso", "accountId": "ContosoAccountId", "lastModifierUserName": "SampleUserName", "created": "2023-04-25T14:59:52.7433333", "lastModified": "2023-04-25T14:59:52.7433333", [property dropdown] [value dropdown] }. Property dropdown options: "enabled": / "state": / "tags": / "useBuiltin":. Value dropdown options: ["Excluded"] / ["Included"] / false / true.
correctText: Property = "enabled": ; Value = false. (i.e. "enabled": false)
explanation: Setting the brand's enabled property to false excludes that custom brand from being detected by Video Indexer.

question: You have a local folder that contains the files shown in the following table. Name | Format | Length (mins) | Size (MB): File1 | WMV | 34 | 400; File2 | AVI | 90 | 1,200; File3 | MOV | 300 | 980; File4 | MP4 | 80 | 1,800. You need to analyze the files by using Azure AI Video Indexer. Which files can you upload to the Video Indexer website?
options: B. File1, File2, File3 and File4
C. File1, File2, and File3 only
E. File1, File2, and File4 only
correctText: B. File1, File2, File3 and File4
explanation: The Video Indexer website supports WMV, AVI, MOV and MP4 formats and a 2-GB per-file size limit, so all four files (each under 2 GB) can be uploaded.
mlTitle: Video Indexer upload and index videos

question: Note: This question is part of a series of questions that present the same scenario. You build a language model by using a Language Understanding service. The language model is used to search for information on a contact list by using an intent named FindContact. A conversational expert provides you with the following list of phrases to use for training: Find contacts in London; Who do I know in Seattle?; Search for contacts in Ukraine. You need to implement the phrase list in Language Understanding. Solution: You create a new pattern in the FindContact intent. Does this meet the goal?
correctText: A. Yes
explanation: Patterns let you define example phrases (templates) for an intent to improve prediction, so creating a new pattern in the FindContact intent is a valid way to implement the phrase list.

question: Note: This question is part of a series of questions that present the same scenario. You develop an application to identify species of flowers by training a Custom Vision model. You receive images of new flower species. You need to add the new images to the classifier. Solution: You add the new images, and then use the Smart Labeler tool. Does this meet the goal?
correctText: B. No
explanation: Smart Labeler only suggests tags from tags the model already knows; for brand-new species it cannot label unknown classes, so this alone does not meet the goal.

question: Note: This question is part of a series of questions that present the same scenario. You develop an application to identify species of flowers by training a Custom Vision model. You receive images of new flower species. You need to add the new images to the classifier. Solution: You add the new images and labels to the existing model. You retrain the model, and then publish the model. Does this meet the goal?
correctText: A. Yes
explanation: Adding the new images with labels to the existing model and then retraining and publishing correctly extends the classifier to the new species.

question: Note: This question is part of a series of questions that present the same scenario. You develop an application to identify species of flowers by training a Custom Vision model. You receive images of new flower species. You need to add the new images to the classifier. Solution: You create a new model, and then upload the new images and labels. Does this meet the goal?
correctText: B. No
explanation: Creating a brand-new model discards the existing trained classifier; you should add the new images/labels to the existing model and retrain instead.

question: HOTSPOT - You are developing a service that records lectures given in English (United Kingdom). You have a method named AppendToTranscriptFile that takes translated text and a language identifier. You need to develop code that will provide transcripts of the lectures to attendees in their respective language. The supported languages are English, French, Spanish, and German. Code: var config = SpeechTranslationConfig.FromSubscription("...", "uksouth"); var lang = new List<string> [DROPDOWN 1]; config.SpeechRecognitionLanguage = "en-GB"; lang.ForEach(config.AddTargetLanguage); using var audioConfig = AudioConfig.FromDefaultMicrophoneInput(); using var recognizer = new [DROPDOWN 2](config, audioConfig); var result = await recognizer.RecognizeOnceAsync(); if (result.Reason == ResultReason.TranslatedSpeech). Dropdown 1 options: {"en-GB"} / {"fr", "de", "es"} / {"French", "Spanish", "German"} / {languages}. Dropdown 2 options: IntentRecognizer / SpeakerRecognizer / SpeechSynthesizer / TranslationRecognizer.
correctText: Dropdown 1 = {"fr", "de", "es"}; Dropdown 2 = TranslationRecognizer.
explanation: The target languages (French, German, Spanish) are added via AddTargetLanguage using their language codes fr/de/es, and speech translation is performed with a TranslationRecognizer.

question: DRAG DROP - You train a Custom Vision model used in a mobile app. You receive 1,000 new images that do not have any associated data. You need to use the images to retrain the model. The solution must minimize how long it takes to retrain the model. Which three actions should you perform in the Custom Vision portal? Actions: Upload the images by category; Get suggested tags; Upload all the images; Group the images locally into category folders; Review the suggestions and confirm the tags.
options: Review the suggestions and confirm the tags.
order: Upload all the images
Get suggested tags
Review the suggestions and confirm the tags
correctText: 1. Upload all the images. 2. Get suggested tags. 3. Review the suggestions and confirm the tags.
explanation: Upload all 1,000 unlabeled images, use the Smart Labeler to get suggested tags, then review and confirm the suggestions to label them quickly before retraining.

question: You are building a Conversational Language Understanding model for an e-commerce chatbot. Users can speak or type their billing address when prompted by the chatbot. You need to construct an entity to capture billing addresses. Which entity type should you use?
correctText: A. machine learned
explanation: A billing address is free-form and contextual, so a machine-learned entity (which learns from labeled examples in context) is the appropriate CLU entity type.

question: You are building an Azure WebJob that will create knowledge bases from an array of URLs. You instantiate a QnAMakerClient object that has the relevant API keys and assign the object to a variable named client. You need to develop a method to create the knowledge bases. Which two actions should you include in the method? Each correct answer presents part of the solution.
options: A. Create a list of FileDTO objects that represents data from the WebJob.
C. Create a list of QnADTO objects that represents data from the WebJob.
correctText: B. Call the client.Knowledgebase.CreateAsync method; D. Create a CreateKbDTO object.
explanation: To create a QnA Maker knowledge base you build a CreateKbDTO (which can include the source URLs) and pass it to client.Knowledgebase.CreateAsync.

question: HOTSPOT - You are developing an application that includes language translation. The application will translate text retrieved by using a function named getTextToBeTranslated. The text can be in one of many languages. The content of the text must remain within the Americas Azure geography. You need to develop code to translate the text to a single language. Code: var endpoint = [DROPDOWN 1]; var apikey = "..."; var text = getTextToBeTranslated(); var body = "[{\"Text\":\"" + text + "\"}]"; var client = new HttpClient(); client.DefaultRequestHeaders.Add("Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key", apikey); [DROPDOWN 2]; HttpResponseMessage response; var content = new StringContent(body, Encoding.UTF8, "application/json"); var response = await client.PutAsync(uri, content). Dropdown 1 options: 'https://api.cognitive.microsofttranslator.com/translate' / '.../transliterate' / 'https://api-apc.cognitive.microsofttranslator.com/detect' / 'https://api-nam.cognitive.microsofttranslator.com/detect' / 'https://api-nam.cognitive.microsofttranslator.com/translate'. Dropdown 2 options: var uri = endpoint + "?from=..." / var uri = endpoint + "?suggestedFrom=..." / var uri = endpoint + "?to=en".
correctText: Dropdown 1 = "https://api-nam.cognitive.microsofttranslator.com/translate"; Dropdown 2 = var uri = endpoint + "?to=en";
explanation: The api-nam endpoint keeps processing within the Americas geography, and ?to=en translates the detected source language into a single target language (English).

question: You are building a conversational language understanding model. You need to enable active learning. What should you do?
options: A. Add show-allintents=true to the prediction endpoint query.
C. Add log=true to the prediction endpoint query.
correctText: C. Add log=true to the prediction endpoint query.
explanation: Active learning requires logging real user queries; adding log=true to the prediction endpoint query captures utterances for review and suggested improvements.
mlTitle: LUIS active learning / review endpoint utterances

question: HOTSPOT - You run the following command: docker run --rm -it -p 5000:5000 --memory 10g --cpus 2 mcr.microsoft.com/azure-cognitive-services/textanalytics/sentiment Eula=accept Billing={ENDPOINT_URI} ApiKey={API_KEY}. For each statement, select Yes if true, otherwise No. Statements: (1) Going to http://localhost:5000/status will query the Azure endpoint to verify whether the API key used to start the container is valid. (2) The container logging provider will write log data. (3) Going to http://localhost:5000/swagger will provide the details to access the documentation for the available endpoints.
correctText: (1) /status verifies the API key = Yes; (2) The container logging provider will write log data = No; (3) /swagger provides API documentation = Yes.
explanation: The /status endpoint validates the container's API key and /swagger exposes the API documentation; container logging is not enabled by default unless logging settings are configured.

question: You are building a Language Understanding model for an e-commerce platform. You need to construct an entity to capture billing addresses. Which entity type should you use for the billing address?
correctText: A. machine learned
explanation: A billing address is a complex, variable, free-form value best captured by a machine-learned entity trained on labeled examples.

question: You need to upload speech samples to a Speech Studio project for use in training. How should you upload the samples?
options: A. Combine the speech samples into a single audio file in the .wma format and upload the file.
B. Upload a zip file that contains a collection of audio files in the .wav format and a corresponding text transcript file.
C. Upload individual audio files in the FLAC format and manually upload a corresponding transcript in Microsoft Word format.
D. Upload individual audio files in the .wma format.
correctText: B. Upload a zip file that contains a collection of audio files in the .wav format and a corresponding text transcript file.
explanation: Custom Speech training data is uploaded as a .zip of .wav audio files plus a matching plain-text transcript file.

question: You are developing a method for an application that uses the Translator API. The method will receive the content of a webpage, and then translate the content into Greek (el). The result will also contain a transliteration that uses the Roman alphabet. You need to create the URI for the call to the Translator API. You have the following URI: https://api.cognitive.microsofttranslator.com/translate?api-version=3.0. Which three additional query parameters should you include in the URI? Each correct answer presents part of the solution.
correctText: C. textType=html; D. to=el; F. toScript=Latn
explanation: textType=html because the input is webpage content, to=el targets Greek, and toScript=Latn returns the transliteration in the Roman (Latin) alphabet.

question: You have a chatbot that was built by using the Microsoft Bot Framework. You need to debug the chatbot endpoint remotely. Which two tools should you install on a local computer? Each correct answer presents part of the solution.
correctText: C. Bot Framework Emulator; E. ngrok
explanation: ngrok exposes the local endpoint to the internet for remote/inspectable traffic, and the Bot Framework Emulator connects to and debugs the bot endpoint.

question: DRAG DROP - You are building a retail chatbot that will use a QnA Maker service. You upload an internal support document to train the model. The document contains the question: 'What is your warranty period?'. Users report the chatbot returns the default QnA Maker answer when they ask: 'How long is the warranty coverage?'. The chatbot returns the correct answer when users ask 'What is your warranty period?'. Both questions should return the same answer. You need to increase the accuracy of the chatbot responses. Which three actions should you perform in sequence? Actions: Add a new question and answer (QnA) pair; Retrain the model; Add additional questions to the document; Republish the model; Add alternative phrasing to the question and answer (QnA) pair.
options: Add alternative phrasing to the question and answer (QnA) pair.
order: Add alternative phrasing to the question and answer (QnA) pair
Retrain the model
Republish the model
correctText: 1. Add alternative phrasing to the question and answer (QnA) pair. 2. Retrain the model. 3. Republish the model.
explanation: Adding the paraphrased question as alternative phrasing to the existing QnA pair, then retraining and republishing, makes both questions return the same answer. NOTE: The PDF's red marks show 'Add a new question and answer (QnA) pair' as step 1, but the documented best practice for a paraphrase is to add alternative phrasing to the existing pair.

question: Note: This question is part of a series of questions that present the same scenario. You build a language model by using a Language Understanding service. The language model is used to search for information on a contact list by using an intent named FindContact. A conversational expert provides you with the following list of phrases to use for training: Find contacts in London; Who do I know in Seattle?; Search for contacts in Ukraine. You need to implement the phrase list in Language Understanding. Solution: You create a new intent for location. Does this meet the goal?
correctText: B. No
explanation: The phrases all express the same FindContact intent (the locations are entities, not separate intents), so creating a new 'location' intent does not meet the goal.

question: Note: This question is part of a series of questions that present the same scenario. You build a language model by using a Language Understanding service. The language model is used to search for information on a contact list by using an intent named FindContact. A conversational expert provides you with the following list of phrases to use for training: Find contacts in London; Who do I know in Seattle?; Search for contacts in Ukraine. You need to implement the phrase list in Language Understanding. Solution: You create a new entity for the domain. Does this meet the goal?
correctText: B. No
explanation: Creating a new entity for the domain does not implement the training phrases for the FindContact intent, so it does not meet the goal.

question: You are training a Language Understanding model for a user support system. You create the first intent named GetContactDetails and add 200 examples. You need to decrease the likelihood of a false positive. What should you do?
options: B. Add a machine learned entity.
C. Add additional examples to the GetContactDetails intent.
D. Add examples to the None intent.
correctText: D. Add examples to the None intent.
explanation: Adding out-of-scope utterances to the None intent teaches the model what does not belong to GetContactDetails, reducing false positives.

question: DRAG DROP - You are building a Language Understanding model for purchasing tickets. You have the following utterance for an intent named PurchaseAndSendTickets: Purchase [2 audit business] tickets to [Paris] [next Monday] and send tickets to [email@domain.com]. You need to select the entity types. The solution must use built-in entity types to minimize training data whenever possible. Which entity type should you use for each label? Available entity types: Email, List, Regex, GeographyV2, Machine learned. Labels: Paris; email@domain.com; 2 audit business.
correctText: Paris = GeographyV2; email@domain.com = Email; 2 audit business = Machine learned.
explanation: Paris and the email use the built-in GeographyV2 and Email prebuilt entities, while '2 audit business' (a quantity plus a custom ticket class) requires a machine-learned entity.

question: You have the following C# method: static void create_resource(string resource_name, string kind, string account_tier, string location) { CognitiveServicesAccount parameters = new CognitiveServicesAccount(null, null, kind, location, resource_name, new CognitiveServicesAccountProperties(), new Sku(account_tier)); var result = cog_svc_client.Accounts.Create(resource_group_name, account_tier, parameters); }. You need to deploy an Azure resource to the East US Azure region. The resource will be used to perform sentiment analysis. How should you call the method?
correctText: B. create_resource("res1", "TextAnalytics", "S0", "eastus")
explanation: Sentiment analysis uses the TextAnalytics kind, the pricing tier/SKU is 'S0', and the Azure region location code is 'eastus'.

question: You build a Conversational Language Understanding model by using the Language Services portal. You export the model as a JSON file as shown in the following sample: { "text": "average amount of rain by month at chicago last year", "intent": "Weather.CheckWeatherValue", "entities": [ { "entity": "Weather.WeatherRange", "startPos": 0, "endPos": 6, "children": [] }, { "entity": "Weather.WeatherCondition", "startPos": 18, "endPos": 21, "children": [] }, { "entity": "Weather.Historic", "startPos": 23, "endPos": 30, "children": [] } ] }. To what does the Weather.Historic entity correspond in the utterance?
correctText: A. by month
explanation: Character positions 23-30 in 'average amount of rain by month at chicago last year' span 'by month'.

question: You are examining the Text Analytics output of an application. The text analyzed is: 'Our tour guide took us up the Space Needle during our trip to Seattle last week.' The response contains the data shown in the following table. Text | Category | ConfidenceScore: Tour guide | PersonType | 0.45; Space Needle | Location | 0.38; Trip | Event | 0.78; Seattle | Location | 0.78; Last week | DateTime | 0.80. Which Text Analytics API is used to analyze the text?
correctText: B. Named Entity Recognition
explanation: The output assigns categories such as PersonType, Location, Event and DateTime to spans of text, which is the Named Entity Recognition (NER) feature.

question: SIMULATION - You need to configure and publish bot12345678 to support task management. The intent must be named TaskReminder. The LUDown for the intent is in the C:\Resources\LU folder. To complete this task, use the Microsoft Bot Framework Composer.
correctText: Hands-on simulation: In Bot Framework Composer, open bot12345678, import the LUDown (.lu) file from C:\Resources\LU to create the TaskReminder intent, add a trigger/dialog for it, then publish the bot.
explanation: This is a hands-on simulation task (no multiple-choice answer); use Bot Framework Composer to import the LU file, create the TaskReminder intent, and publish the bot.

question: SIMULATION - You need to configure bot12345678 to support the French (FR-FR) language. Export the bot to C:\Resources\Bot\Bot1.zip. To complete this task, use the Microsoft Bot Framework Composer.
correctText: Hands-on simulation: In Bot Framework Composer, add the French (fr-fr) language to the bot's languages/localization settings, then export the bot as a .zip to C:\Resources\Bot\Bot1.zip.
explanation: This is a hands-on simulation task (no multiple-choice answer); enable the fr-fr language in Composer's language settings and export the bot.

question: SIMULATION - You need to configure and publish bot12345678 to answer questions by using the frequently asked questions (FAQ) located at https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/bot-service/bot-service-resources-bot-framework-faq. The solution must use bot[lab].LabInstance.Id-qna-qna. To complete this task, use the Microsoft Bot Framework Composer.
correctText: Hands-on simulation: In Bot Framework Composer, add a QnA Maker knowledge base populated from the FAQ URL (using the specified qna resource name), wire it into the bot, then publish.
explanation: This is a hands-on simulation task (no multiple-choice answer); create/import a QnA knowledge base from the FAQ URL in Composer and publish the bot.
mlTitle: Add QnA to a bot in Composer

question: You need to measure the public perception of your brand on social media by using natural language processing. Which Azure service should you use?
correctText: A. Language service
explanation: Measuring public perception (positive/negative opinion) is sentiment analysis, which is provided by the Azure AI Language service.

question:
HOTSPOT - You are developing an application that includes language translation. The application will translate text retrieved by using a function named get_text_to_be_translated. The text can be in one of many languages. The content of the text must remain within the Americas Azure geography. You need to develop code to translate the text to a single language. Complete the code:
api_key = "..."
text = get_text_to_be_translated()
headers = { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', 'Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key': api_key }
body = { 'Text': text }
conn = httplib.HTTPSConnection([DROPDOWN 1])
conn.request("POST", [DROPDOWN 2], str(body), headers)
Dropdown 1 options: "api.cognitive.microsofttranslator.com" | "api-apc.cognitive.microsofttranslator.com" | "api-nam.cognitive.microsofttranslator.com". Dropdown 2 options: "/translate?from=en" | "/translate?suggestedFrom=en" | "/translate?to=en" | "/detect?to=en" | "/detect?from=en".
correctText: Dropdown 1 (HTTPSConnection host): "api-nam.cognitive.microsofttranslator.com". Dropdown 2 (request path): "/translate?to=en".
explanation: Data sovereignty in the Americas requires the NAM (North America) regional endpoint api-nam.cognitive.microsofttranslator.com. Because the source text can be any language, you translate to a single language with /translate?to=en and let the service auto-detect the source.

question: You have the following data sources: Finance: On-premises Microsoft SQL Server database; Sales: Azure Cosmos DB using the Core (SQL) API; Logs: Azure Table storage; HR: Azure SQL database. You need to ensure that you can search all the data by using the Azure Cognitive Search REST API. What should you do?
options: A. Migrate the data in HR to Azure Blob storage
B. Migrate the data in HR to the on-premises SQL server.
C. Export the data in Finance to Azure Data Lake Storage.
D. Ingest the data in Logs into Azure Sentinel
correctText: C. Export the data in Finance to Azure Data Lake Storage.
explanation: Azure Cognitive Search indexers cannot reach the on-premises SQL Server (Finance). Cosmos DB SQL API, Azure Table storage and Azure SQL are all supported indexer data sources, so only Finance must be moved to a supported cloud source such as Azure Data Lake Storage.

question: SIMULATION - Task: You need to create and publish a Language Understanding (classic) model named lu12345678. The model will contain an intent of Travel that has an utterance of 'Boat'. To complete this task, sign in to the Language Understanding portal at https://www.luis.ai/.
correctText: In the LUIS portal: create a new app named lu12345678, create an intent named Travel, add the utterance 'Boat' to the Travel intent, then Train and Publish the app.
explanation: Lab simulation. In the LUIS (luis.ai) portal you create the app, add the Travel intent with the 'Boat' utterance, train, and publish the model.

question: SIMULATION - Task: You need to create a version of the lu12345678 Language Understanding (classic) model. The new version must have a version name of 1.0 and must be active. To complete this task, sign in to the Language Understanding portal at https://www.luis.ai/.
correctText: In the LUIS portal open the lu12345678 app, use Manage > Versions > Clone version, name the new version 1.0, then set it as the active version.
explanation: Lab simulation. Clone a version of the app under Manage > Versions, name it 1.0, and mark it active.

question: You have a Language service resource that performs the following: Sentiment analysis; Named Entity Recognition (NER); Personally Identifiable Information (PII) identification. You need to prevent the resource from persisting input data once the data is analyzed. Which query parameter in the Language service API should you configure?
correctText: D. loggingOptOut
explanation: The loggingOptOut query parameter opts out of having input data temporarily stored/logged by the service, preventing the resource from persisting the analyzed input.
mlTitle: Azure AI Language service data and limits

question: You have an Azure Cognitive Services model named Model1 that identifies the intent of text input. You develop an app in C# named App1. You need to configure App1 to use Model1. Which package should you add to App1?
correctText: C. Azure.AI.Language.Conversations
explanation: Intent recognition from text is provided by Conversational Language Understanding (CLU). The C# client library for CLU is the Azure.AI.Language.Conversations NuGet package.

question: HOTSPOT - You are building content for a video training solution. You need to create narration to accompany the video content. The solution must use Custom Neural Voice. What should you use to create a custom neural voice, and which service should you use to generate the narration? Custom neural voice options: Microsoft Bot Framework Composer | The Azure portal | The Language Understanding portal | The Speech Studio portal. Narration options: Language Understanding | Speaker Recognition | Speech-to-text | Text-to-speech.
correctText: Custom neural voice: The Speech Studio portal. Narration: Text-to-speech.
explanation: Custom Neural Voice models are created and trained in the Speech Studio portal. Narration (synthesized speech) is generated using the Text-to-speech service.

question: HOTSPOT - You are building a call handling system that will receive calls from French-speaking and German-speaking callers. The system must: Capture inbound voice messages as text; Replay messages in English on demand. Which Azure Cognitive Services services should you use? To capture messages options: Speaker Recognition | Speech-to-text | Text-to-speech | Translator. To replay messages options: Speech-to-text only | Speech-to-text and Language | Speaker Recognition and Language | Text-to-speech and Language | Text-to-speech and Translator.
correctText: To capture messages: Speech-to-text. To replay messages: Text-to-speech and Translator.
explanation: Capturing inbound voice as text uses Speech-to-text. Replaying the French/German messages in English requires translating the text (Translator) and synthesizing speech (Text-to-speech).

question: You are building a social media extension that will convert text to speech. The solution must: Support messages of up to 400 characters; Provide users with multiple voice options; Minimize costs. You create an Azure Cognitive Services resource. Which Speech API endpoint provides users with the available voice options?
correctText: C. https://uksouth.tts.speech.microsoft.com/cognitiveservices/voices/list
explanation: The text-to-speech 'voices/list' endpoint returns the full list of standard and neural voices available for the region, letting users choose from multiple voice options.

question: You develop a custom question answering project in Azure Cognitive Service for Language. The project will be used by a chatbot. You need to configure the project to engage in multi-turn conversations. What should you do?
correctText: A. Add follow-up prompts.
explanation: Follow-up prompts create multi-turn conversation flows in custom question answering, allowing the bot to ask clarifying follow-up questions before giving an answer.

question: HOTSPOT - You are building a solution that students will use to find references for essays. You use code that calls client.RecognizeLinkedEntities("Our tour guide took us up the Space Needle during our trip to Seattle last week."). For each statement select Yes or No: 1) The code will detect the language of documents. 2) The url attribute returned for each linked entity will be a Bing search link. 3) The matches attribute returned for each linked entity will provide the location in a document where the entity is referenced.
correctText: 1) No  2) No  3) Yes
explanation: RecognizeLinkedEntities does not detect language. The url attribute links to a data source such as Wikipedia, not a Bing search. The matches attribute gives the offset/length where each entity appears in the document.

question: You train a Conversational Language Understanding model to understand the natural language input of users. You need to evaluate the accuracy of the model before deploying it. What are two methods you can use? Each correct answer presents a complete solution.
options: A. From the language authoring REST endpoint, retrieve the model evaluation summary.
B. From Language Studio, enable Active Learning, and then validate the utterances logged for review.
C. From Language Studio, select Model performance.
D. From the Azure portal, enable log collection in Log Analytics, and then analyze the logs
correctText: A. From the language authoring REST endpoint, retrieve the model evaluation summary. C. From Language Studio, select Model performance.
explanation: CLU model accuracy is evaluated either via the authoring REST API model evaluation summary (precision/recall/F1) or the Model performance view in Language Studio. Active Learning and Log Analytics do not evaluate accuracy before deployment.

correctText: Blank 1 (= "en-US"): SpeechRecognitionLanguage. Blank 2 ("de"): AddTargetLanguage.
explanation: The source language English is set with SpeechRecognitionLanguage = "en-US", and the German target is added with AddTargetLanguage("de").
mlTitle: Translate speech with the Speech SDK

question: You have an Azure subscription that contains an Azure Cognitive Service for Language resource. You need to identify the URL of the REST interface for the Language service. Which blade should you use in the Azure portal?
correctText: B. Keys and Endpoint
explanation: The Keys and Endpoint blade of a Cognitive Services / Language resource displays the REST endpoint URL along with the access keys.

question: DRAG DROP - You are building a transcription service for technical podcasts. Testing reveals that the service fails to transcribe technical terms accurately. You need to improve the accuracy of the service. Which five actions should you perform in sequence? Actions: Deploy the model; Create a Custom Speech project; Upload training datasets; Create a speech-to-text model; Create a Speaker Recognition model; Train the model; Create a Conversational Language Understanding model.
order: Create a Custom Speech project
Create a speech-to-text model
Upload training datasets
Train the model
Deploy the model
correctText: 1) Create a Custom Speech project. 2) Create a speech-to-text model. 3) Upload training datasets. 4) Train the model. 5) Deploy the model.
explanation: Custom Speech workflow: create a project, create a speech-to-text (custom) model, upload training data, train the model, then deploy it to improve recognition of technical terms.

question: You are building a retail kiosk system that will use a custom neural voice. You acquire audio samples and consent from the voice talent. You need to create a voice talent profile. What should you upload to the profile?
options: A. a zip file that contains 10-second .wav files and the associated transcripts as .txt files
B. a five-minute .flac audio file and the associated transcript as a .txt file
C. a .wav or .mp3 file of the voice talent consenting to the creation of a synthetic version of their voice
D. a five-minute .wav or .mp3 file of the voice talent describing the kiosk system
correctText: C. a .wav or .mp3 file of the voice talent consenting to the creation of a synthetic version of their voice
explanation: The voice talent profile requires a recorded consent statement (a .wav or .mp3 file) in which the talent agrees to creation of a synthetic version of their voice.

question: DRAG DROP - You have a Language Understanding solution that runs in a Docker container. You download the Language Understanding container image from the Microsoft Container Registry (MCR). You need to deploy the container image to a host computer. Which three actions should you perform in sequence? Actions: From the host computer, move the package file to the Docker input directory; From the Language Understanding portal, export the solution as a package file; From the host computer, build the container and specify the output directory; From the host computer, run the container and specify the input directory; From the Language Understanding portal, retrain the model.
order: From the Language Understanding portal, export the solution as a package file
From the host computer, move the package file to the Docker input directory
From the host computer, run the container and specify the input directory
correctText: 1) From the Language Understanding portal, export the solution as a package file. 2) From the host computer, move the package file to the Docker input directory. 3) From the host computer, run the container and specify the input directory.
explanation: Export the LUIS app as a package, place it in the container's input directory on the host, then run the container pointing at that input directory.

question:
HOTSPOT - You are building a text-to-speech app that will use a custom neural voice. You need to create an SSML file. The voice profile must Express a calm tone and Imitate the voice of a young adult female. Complete the code:
<mstts:express-as [BLANK 1]="YoungAdultFemale" [BLANK 2]="gentle"> How can I assist you? </mstts:express-as>
Each blank options: role | style | styledegree | type | voice.
correctText: Blank 1 (="YoungAdultFemale"): role. Blank 2 (="gentle"): style.
explanation: In mstts:express-as the role attribute makes the voice imitate a persona such as YoungAdultFemale, while the style attribute sets the speaking style/tone such as gentle (calm).

question: You are building an Azure AI Language Understanding solution. You discover that many intents have similar utterances containing airport names or airport codes. You need to minimize the number of utterances used to train the model. Which type of custom entity should you use?
correctText: D. list
explanation: A list entity matches an explicit, closed set of values (airport names and their codes/synonyms), so the model recognizes them without needing many training utterances.

question: You have a text-based chatbot. You need to enable content moderation by using the Text Moderation API of Content Moderator. Which two service responses should you use? Each correct answer presents part of the solution.
correctText: A. personal data. C. text classification.
explanation: The Text Moderation API returns personal data (PII) detection and text classification (offensive/category scores). Adult/racy classification scores and OCR belong to the Image Moderation API, not text moderation.

question: HOTSPOT - You have a function GetKeyWords that calls textAnalyticsClient.RecognizeEntities(text) and writes each entity.Text. You call it with the string 'Our tour of Paris included a visit to the Eiffel Tower'. For each statement select Yes or No: 1) The output will include the following words: our and included. 2) The output will include the following words: Paris, Eiffel, and Tower. 3) The function will output all the key phrases from the input string to the console.
correctText: 1) No  2) Yes  3) No
explanation: RecognizeEntities returns named entities. 'our' and 'included' are not entities (No). 'Paris' and 'Eiffel Tower' are recognized location entities (Yes). The method outputs entities, not all key phrases (No).

question:
HOTSPOT - You are building an Azure web app named App1 that will translate text from English to Spanish. You need to use the Text Translation REST API. The solution must ensure data sovereignty in the United States. Complete the URI:
https://[BLANK 1]/[BLANK 2]?api-version=3.0&to=es
Blank 1 options: api.cognitive.microsofttranslator.com | api-nam.cognitive.microsofttranslator.com | api-nam.cognitiveservices.azure.com | eastus.api.cognitive.microsoft.com. Blank 2 options: detect | languages | text-to-speech | translate.
correctText: Blank 1 (host): api-nam.cognitive.microsofttranslator.com. Blank 2 (path): translate.
explanation: US data sovereignty requires the North America regional endpoint api-nam.cognitive.microsofttranslator.com, and text translation uses the /translate path.

question: DRAG DROP - You have a Docker host named Host1 that contains a container base image. You have an Azure subscription that contains a custom speech-to-text model named model1. You need to run model1 on Host1. Which three actions should you perform in sequence? Actions: Retrain the model; Request approval to run the container; Export model1 to Host1; Run the container; Configure disk logging.
order: Request approval to run the container
Export model1 to Host1
Run the container
correctText: 1) Request approval to run the container. 2) Export model1 to Host1. 3) Run the container.
explanation: Speech containers are gated, so first request approval to run the container, then export the custom model to the host, then run the container.

question: Note: This question is part of a series that present the same scenario. You build a language model by using Conversational Language Understanding. The language model is used to search for information on a contact list by using an intent named FindContact. A conversational expert provides phrases for training: 'Find contacts in London.', 'Who do I know in Seattle?', 'Search for contacts in Ukraine.' You need to implement the phrase list in Conversational Language Understanding. Solution: You create a new utterance for each phrase in the FindContact intent. Does this meet the goal?
correctText: A. Yes
explanation: Adding each phrase as an utterance under the FindContact intent is a valid way to train the intent on the supplied phrase list, so the solution meets the goal.

question: DRAG DROP - You have a question answering project in Azure Cognitive Service for Language. You need to move the project to a Language service instance in a different Azure region. Which three actions should you perform in sequence? Actions: From the new Language service instance, train and publish the project; From the new Language service instance, import the project file; From the new Language service instance, enable custom text classification; From the original Language service instance, export the existing project; From the new Language service instance, regenerate the keys.
order: From the original Language service instance, export the existing project
From the new Language service instance, import the project file
From the new Language service instance, train and publish the project
correctText: 1) From the original Language service instance, export the existing project. 2) From the new Language service instance, import the project file. 3) From the new Language service instance, train and publish the project.
explanation: To migrate a question answering project: export it from the source instance, import the exported file into the new-region instance, then train and publish it there.

question: DRAG DROP - You are building a customer support chatbot. You need to configure the bot to identify: Code names for internal product development; Messages that include credit card numbers. The solution must minimize development effort. Which Azure Cognitive Service for Language feature should you use for each requirement? Features: Custom named entity recognition (NER); Key phrase extraction; Language detection; Named Entity Recognition (NER); Personally identifiable information (PII) detection; Sentiment analysis.
correctText: Identify code names for internal product development: Custom named entity recognition (NER). Identify messages that include credit card numbers: Personally identifiable information (PII) detection.
explanation: Proprietary code names are not in the prebuilt entity set, so a Custom NER model is required. Credit card numbers are a built-in PII category, so out-of-the-box PII detection identifies them with minimal effort.

correctText: Blank 1 (= "fr"): SpeechRecognitionLanguage. Blank 2 ("de"): AddTargetLanguage.
explanation: The source French is set with SpeechRecognitionLanguage = "fr"; the German output is added with AddTargetLanguage("de").
mlTitle: Translate speech with the Speech SDK

question: DRAG DROP - You have a collection of Microsoft Word documents and PowerPoint presentations in German. You need to translate the files to French. Requirements: Preserve the original formatting; Support the use of a custom glossary. You create a blob container for German files and a blob container for French files and upload the originals to the German container. Which three actions should you perform in sequence? Actions: Perform an asynchronous translation by using the list of files to be translated; Perform an asynchronous translation by using the document translation specification; Generate a list of files to be translated; Upload a glossary file to the container for German files; Upload a glossary file to the container for French files; Define a document translation specification that has a French target.
order: Upload a glossary file to the container for French files
Define a document translation specification that has a French target
Perform an asynchronous translation by using the document translation specification
correctText: 1) Upload a glossary file to the container for French files. 2) Define a document translation specification that has a French target. 3) Perform an asynchronous translation by using the document translation specification.
explanation: Document Translation with a glossary: place the glossary in the target (French) container, define a translation specification targeting French (with the glossary), then run the asynchronous batch translation using that specification. This preserves formatting.

question: You have a C# function MyFunction that calls textAnalyticsClient.ExtractKeyPhrases(text) and writes each key phrase. You call MyFunction(textAnalyticsClient, "the quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog"). Which output will you receive?
options: B. the quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog
D. quick brown fox / lazy dog
correctText: D. quick brown fox / lazy dog
explanation: Key phrase extraction returns the meaningful noun phrases. For this sentence the key phrases are 'quick brown fox' and 'lazy dog'.

question: You have the Python method def create_resource(resource_name, kind, account_tier, location): parameters = CognitiveServicesAccount(sku=Sku(name=account_tier), kind=kind, location=location, properties={}); result = cogSvcClient.accounts.create(resource_group_name, resource_name, parameters). You need to deploy an Azure resource to the East US Azure region. The resource will be used to perform sentiment analysis. How should you call the method?
correctText: D. create_resource("res1", "TextAnalytics", "S0", "eastus")
explanation: Sentiment analysis uses the TextAnalytics kind. The SKU name must be a valid value such as S0, and the location parameter must be the region's short, lowercase, no-space form 'eastus'.
mlTitle: Create an Azure AI services resource

question:
DRAG DROP - You develop a Python app named App1 that performs speech-to-speech translation. You need to configure App1 to translate English to German. Complete the SpeechTranslationConfig object:
def translate_speech_to_text():
  translation_config = speechsdk.translation.SpeechTranslationConfig(subscription=speech_key, region=service_region)
  translation_config.[BLANK 1] = "en-US"
  translation_config.[BLANK 2]("de")
Values: add_target_language | speech_synthesis_language | speech_recognition_language | voice_name.
correctText: Blank 1 (= "en-US"): speech_recognition_language. Blank 2 ("de"): add_target_language.
explanation: Source English is set with speech_recognition_language = "en-US"; the German target is added with add_target_language("de").
mlTitle: Translate speech with the Speech SDK

question:
HOTSPOT - You are developing a streaming Speech to Text solution that will use the Speech SDK and MP3 encoding. You need to convert speech to text for streaming MP3 data. Complete the code:
audio_format = speechsdk.audio.[BLANK 1](compressed_stream_format=speechsdk.AudioStreamContainerFormat.MP3)
...
recognizer = speechsdk.[BLANK 2](speech_config=speech_config, audio_config=audio_config)
Blank 1 options: AudioConfig.SetProperty | AudioStreamFormat | GetWaveFormatPCM | PullAudioInputStream. Blank 2 options: KeywordRecognizer | SpeakerRecognizer | SpeechRecognizer | SpeechSynthesizer.
correctText: Blank 1: AudioStreamFormat. Blank 2: SpeechRecognizer.
explanation: AudioStreamFormat with a compressed_stream_format (MP3) defines the compressed input audio format, and SpeechRecognizer performs the speech-to-text recognition.

question: HOTSPOT - You are building a chatbot. You need to use the Content Moderator API to identify aggressive and sexually explicit language. Which three settings should you configure in the Content Moderator - Moderate Text - Screen operation? Settings include Host, Resource Name, query parameters (autocorrect, PII, listId, classify, language), and headers (Content-Type, Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key).
correctText: 1) Resource Name (host). 2) classify (query parameter). 3) Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key (header).
explanation: You must set the resource name in the host, set classify=true so the API classifies text into offensive categories, and provide the Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key for authentication.

question: You are developing an app that will use the Decision and Language APIs. You need to provision resources for the app. The solution must ensure that each service is accessed by using a single endpoint and credential. Which type of resource should you create?
correctText: C. Azure Cognitive Services
explanation: A multi-service Azure Cognitive Services resource exposes multiple services (including Decision and Language) through a single endpoint and key.

question: You are building a chatbot. You need to ensure that the bot will recognize the names of your company's products and codenames. The solution must minimize development effort. Which Azure Cognitive Service for Language service should you include in the solution?
correctText: C. custom Named Entity Recognition (NER)
explanation: Product names and codenames are proprietary entities not in the prebuilt model, so a custom NER model trained on those entities is required.

question: You have an Azure subscription that contains an Azure App Service app named App1. You provision a multi-service Azure Cognitive Services resource named CSAccount1. You need to configure App1 to access CSAccount1. The solution must minimize administrative effort. What should you use to configure App1?
options: A. a system-assigned managed identity and an X.509 certificate
B. the endpoint URI and an OAuth token
C. the endpoint URI and a shared access signature (SAS) token
D. the endpoint URI and subscription key
correctText: D. the endpoint URI and subscription key
explanation: The simplest way to authenticate to a Cognitive Services resource is the endpoint URI plus the subscription (account) key, which minimizes administrative effort.
mlTitle: Authenticate requests to Azure AI services

question: You have an Azure subscription that contains a multi-service Azure Cognitive Services Translator resource named Translator1. You are building an app that will translate text and documents by using Translator1. You need to create the REST API request for the app. Which headers should you include in the request?
options: A. the access control request, the content type, and the content length
B. the subscription key and the client trace ID
C. the resource ID and the content language
D. the subscription key, the subscription region, and the content type
correctText: D. the subscription key, the subscription region, and the content type
explanation: Calling Translator through a multi-service resource requires the Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key, the Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Region, and Content-Type headers.

question: You have a file share that contains 5,000 images of scanned invoices. You need to analyze the images. The solution must extract the following data: Invoice items; Sales amounts; Customer details. What should you use?
correctText: D. Azure AI Document Intelligence
explanation: Azure AI Document Intelligence has a prebuilt invoice model that extracts line items, totals/sales amounts, and customer details from scanned invoices.

question: HOTSPOT - You have a Python function get_key_words that calls textAnalyticsClient.recognize_entities(documents=[text])[0] and prints each entity.text. You call it with the string 'Our tour of Paris included a visit to the Eiffel Tower'. For each statement select Yes or No: 1) The output will include the following words: our and included. 2) The output will include the following words: Paris, Eiffel, and Tower. 3) The function will output all the key phrases from the input string to the console.
correctText: 1) No  2) Yes  3) No
explanation: recognize_entities returns named entities. 'our' and 'included' are not entities (No). 'Paris' and 'Eiffel Tower' are recognized location entities (Yes). The function outputs entities, not all key phrases (No).

question: HOTSPOT - You have a Python method get_key_phrases that calls text_analytics_client.extract_key_phrases(text, language="en") and prints each key phrase. You call get_key_phrases(text_analytics_client, "the cat sat on the mat"). For each statement select Yes or No: 1) The call will output key phrases from the input string to the console. 2) The output will contain the following words: the, cat, sat, on, and mat. 3) The output will contain the confidence level for key phrases.
correctText: 1) Yes  2) No  3) No
explanation: extract_key_phrases prints the key phrases (Yes). It returns only the meaningful noun phrases such as 'cat' and 'mat', not every word (No). It does not return confidence scores (No).

question:
HOTSPOT - You are developing a service that records lectures given in English (United Kingdom). You have a method append_to_transcript_file that takes translated text and a language identifier. You need to provide transcripts of the lectures to attendees in English, French, Spanish, and German. Complete the code:
translation_config = speechsdk.translation.SpeechTranslationConfig(subscription=speech_key, region=service_region)
translation_config.speech_recognition_language = "en-GB"
languages = [BLANK 1]
for language in languages: translation_config.add_target_language(language)
...
recognizer = speechsdk.translation.[BLANK 2](translation_config=translation_config, audio_config=audio_config)
Blank 1 options: (['en-GB']) | (['fr','de','es']) | (['French','Spanish','German']) | (['languages']). Blank 2 options: IntentRecognizer | SpeakerRecognizer | SpeechSynthesizer | TranslationRecognizer.
correctText: Blank 1 (languages): ['fr','de','es']. Blank 2 (recognizer): TranslationRecognizer.
explanation: English is the source (speech_recognition_language=en-GB), so the target languages added must be the codes for French, German and Spanish: ['fr','de','es']. A TranslationRecognizer performs the speech translation.
mlTitle: Translate speech with the Speech SDK

question: You are developing an app that will use the text-to-speech capability of the Azure AI Speech service. The app will be used in motor vehicles. You need to optimize the quality of the synthesized voice output. Which Speech Synthesis Markup Language (SSML) attribute should you configure?
options: A. the style attribute of the mstts:express-as element
B. the effect attribute of the voice element
C. the pitch attribute of the prosody element
D. the level attribute of the emphasis element
correctText: B. the effect attribute of the voice element
explanation: The effect attribute of the voice element (e.g. eq_car) applies an audio effect that optimizes the synthesized output for playback in scenarios such as motor vehicles.

question: You are designing a content management system. You need to ensure that the reading experience is optimized for users who have reduced comprehension and learning differences, such as dyslexia. The solution must minimize development effort. Which Azure service should you include in the solution?
correctText: A. Azure AI Immersive Reader
explanation: Immersive Reader is a prebuilt service that improves reading comprehension for users with dyslexia and other learning differences, with minimal development effort.

question: HOTSPOT - You are building an app that will answer customer calls about the status of an order. The app will query a database for the order details and provide the customers with a spoken response. You need to identify which Azure AI service APIs to use. The solution must minimize development effort. Which object should you use for each requirement? Convert customer calls into text queries options: SpeechRecognizer | SpeechSynthesizer | TranslationRecognizer | VoiceProfileClient. Provide customers with the order details options: SpeechRecognizer | SpeechSynthesizer | TranslationRecognizer | VoiceProfileClient.
correctText: Convert customer calls into text queries: SpeechRecognizer. Provide customers with the order details: SpeechSynthesizer.
explanation: Converting spoken calls into text uses SpeechRecognizer (speech-to-text); providing a spoken response uses SpeechSynthesizer (text-to-speech).

question: You have an Azure AI service model named Model1 that identifies the intent of text input. You develop a Python app named App1. You need to configure App1 to use Model1. Which package should you add to App1?
correctText: B. azure-ai-language-conversations
explanation: Intent identification from text uses Conversational Language Understanding. The Python client library is the azure-ai-language-conversations package.

question:
HOTSPOT - You are building an app that will automatically translate speech from English to French, German, and Spanish by using Azure AI service. You need to define the output languages and configure the Azure AI Speech service. Complete the code:
speech_key, service_region = os.environ['SPEECH_SERVICE_KEY'], os.environ['SPEECH_SERVICE_REGION']
languages = [BLANK 1]
def translate_speech_to_text():
  translation_config = speechsdk.translation.SpeechTranslationConfig(...)
  for lang in languages: translation_config.add_target_language(lang)
  recognizer = speechsdk.translation.[BLANK 2](translation_config=translation_config)
Blank 1 options: ['en','fr','de','es'] | ['fr','de','es'] | ['French','Spanish','German']. Blank 2 options: IntentRecognizer | SpeakerRecognizer | SpeechSynthesizer | TranslationRecognizer.
correctText: Blank 1 (languages): ['fr','de','es']. Blank 2 (recognizer): TranslationRecognizer.
explanation: English is the source, so the target languages added are the codes for French, German and Spanish: ['fr','de','es']. A TranslationRecognizer performs the translation.
mlTitle: Translate speech with the Speech SDK

question: DRAG DROP - You plan to implement an Azure AI Search resource that will use a custom skill based on sentiment analysis. You need to create a custom model and configure Azure AI Search to use the model. Which five actions should you perform in sequence? Actions: Create an endpoint for the model; Rerun the indexer to enrich the index; Create an Azure Machine Learning workspace; Create and train the model in the Azure Machine Learning studio; Provision an Azure AI Services resource and obtain the endpoint; Connect the custom skill to the endpoint.
order: Create an Azure Machine Learning workspace
Create and train the model in the Azure Machine Learning studio
Create an endpoint for the model
Connect the custom skill to the endpoint
Rerun the indexer to enrich the index
correctText: 1) Create an Azure Machine Learning workspace. 2) Create and train the model in the Azure Machine Learning studio. 3) Create an endpoint for the model. 4) Connect the custom skill to the endpoint. 5) Rerun the indexer to enrich the index.
explanation: Build the custom model in an Azure ML workspace, train it in ML studio, expose it via an endpoint, connect a custom (Web API) skill to that endpoint, then rerun the indexer so the skillset enriches the index.
mlTitle: Add a custom skill to an AI Search enrichment pipeline

question: HOTSPOT - You have a collection of press releases stored as PDF files. You need to extract text from the files and perform sentiment analysis. Which service should you use for each task? Extract text options: Azure AI Search | Azure AI Vision | Azure AI Document Intelligence. Perform sentiment analysis options: Azure Cognitive Search | Azure AI Computer Vision | Azure AI Document Intelligence | Azure AI Language.
correctText: Extract text: Azure AI Document Intelligence. Perform sentiment analysis: Azure AI Language.
explanation: Azure AI Document Intelligence (Read/Layout) extracts text from PDF files; Azure AI Language performs sentiment analysis on the extracted text.

question: You are building an internet-based training solution. The solution requires that a user's camera and microphone remain enabled. You need to monitor a video stream of the user and verify that the user is alone and is not collaborating with another user. The solution must minimize development effort. What should you include in the solution?
options: A. speech-to-text in the Azure AI Speech service
B. object detection in Azure AI Custom Vision
C. Spatial Analysis in Azure AI Vision
D. object detection in Azure AI Custom Vision
correctText: C. Spatial Analysis in Azure AI Vision
explanation: Spatial Analysis in Azure AI Vision detects the presence and number of people in a video stream, so it can verify a user is alone, with minimal development effort.

question: You are developing an app that will use the Speech and Language APIs. You need to provision resources for the app. The solution must ensure that each service is accessed by using a single endpoint and credential. Which type of resource should you create?
correctText: C. Azure AI Services
explanation: A multi-service Azure AI Services resource provides access to multiple services, including Speech and Language, through a single endpoint and key.

correctText: Blank 1 (languages): {"fr","de","es"}. Blank 2 (recognizer): TranslationRecognizer.
explanation: English is the source, so the list passed to AddTargetLanguage contains only the target codes {"fr","de","es"}. A TranslationRecognizer performs the speech translation.
mlTitle: Translate speech with the Speech SDK

question: You are developing a text processing solution. You have a function GetKeyWords that calls textAnalyticsClient.RecognizeEntities(text) and writes each entity.Text. You call the function with 'Our tour of London included a visit to Buckingham Palace'. What will the function return?
options: A. London and Buckingham Palace only
D. Our tour of London included visit to Buckingham Palace
correctText: A. London and Buckingham Palace only
explanation: RecognizeEntities returns named entities. In this sentence the entities are the locations 'London' and 'Buckingham Palace'; 'tour' and 'visit' are not entities.

question: You have the Python function my_function that calls textAnalyticsClient.extract_key_phrases(documents=[text])[0] and prints each phrase. You call my_function(text_analytics_client, "the quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog"). Following 'Key phrases', what output will you receive?
options: C. quick brown fox / lazy dog
D. the quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog
correctText: C. quick brown fox / lazy dog
explanation: Key phrase extraction returns the meaningful noun phrases, which for this sentence are 'quick brown fox' and 'lazy dog'.

question: You have an Azure subscription. You need to deploy an Azure AI Search resource that will recognize geographic locations. Which built-in skill should you include in the skillset for the resource?
correctText: C. EntityRecognitionSkill
explanation: The EntityRecognitionSkill extracts entities such as Location (geographic places) from text. EntityLinkingSkill only links recognized entities to a knowledge base such as Wikipedia.

question: HOTSPOT - You are developing a text processing solution. You have a C# method GetKeyPhrases that calls textAnalyticsClient.ExtractKeyPhrases(text) and writes each key phrase. You call GetKeyPhrases(textAnalyticsClient, "the cat sat on the mat"). For each statement select Yes or No: 1) The call will output key phrases from the input string to the console. 2) The output will contain the following words: the, cat, sat, on, and mat. 3) The output will contain the confidence level for key phrases.
correctText: 1) Yes  2) No  3) No
explanation: ExtractKeyPhrases prints the key phrases to the console (Yes). It returns only the meaningful noun phrases such as 'cat' and 'mat', not every word (No), and it does not return confidence scores (No).

question: You deploy a web app that is used as a management portal for indexing in Azure Cognitive Search. The app is configured to use the primary admin key. During a security review, you discover unauthorized changes to the search index and suspect the primary access key is compromised. You need to prevent unauthorized access to the index management endpoint. The solution must minimize downtime. What should you do next?
options: A. Regenerate the primary admin key, change the app to use the secondary admin key, and then regenerate the secondary admin key.
B. Change the app to use a query key, and then regenerate the primary admin key and the secondary admin key.
C. Regenerate the secondary admin key, change the app to use the secondary admin key, and then regenerate the primary key.
D. Add a new query key, change the app to use the new query key, and then delete all the unused query keys
correctText: C. Regenerate the secondary admin key, change the app to use the secondary admin key, and then regenerate the primary key.
explanation: First regenerate the (unused) secondary key, switch the app to it so it keeps working, then regenerate the compromised primary key. This invalidates the compromised key with no downtime.
mlTitle: Manage Azure AI Search API keys

question: You have an existing Azure Cognitive Search service. You have an Azure Blob storage account that contains millions of scanned documents stored as images and PDFs. You need to make the scanned documents available to search as quickly as possible. What should you do?
options: A. Split the data into multiple blob containers. Create a Cognitive Search service for each container. Within each indexer definition, schedule the same runtime execution pattern.
B. Split the data into multiple blob containers. Create an indexer for each container. Increase the search units. Within each indexer definition, schedule a sequential execution pattern.
C. Create a Cognitive Search service for each type of document.
D. Split the data into multiple virtual folders. Create an indexer for each folder. Increase the search units. Within each indexer definition, schedule the same runtime execution pattern.
correctText: D. Split the data into multiple virtual folders. Create an indexer for each folder. Increase the search units. Within each indexer definition, schedule the same runtime execution pattern.
explanation: Parallelizing indexing across multiple folders, each with its own indexer running concurrently (same schedule) on a service with more search units, maximizes indexing throughput without creating multiple services.
mlTitle: Index large data sets in Azure AI Search

question: You need to implement a table projection to generate a physical expression of an Azure Cognitive Search index. Which three properties should you specify in the skillset definition JSON configuration table node? Each correct answer presents part of the solution.
correctText: A. tableName. B. generatedKeyName. E. source.
explanation: A table projection node requires tableName (the output table), generatedKeyName (the key column name), and source (the node in the enriched document tree to project).

question: HOTSPOT - You are creating an enrichment pipeline that will use Azure Cognitive Search. The knowledge store contains unstructured JSON data and scanned PDF documents that contain text. Which projection type should you use for each data type? JSON data options: File projection | Object projection | Table projection. Scanned data options: File projection | Object projection | Table projection.
correctText: JSON data: Object projection. Scanned data: File projection.
explanation: Object projections store unstructured JSON documents in blob storage. File projections store binary files such as scanned/normalized images extracted from the source documents.

correctText: 1) Yes  2) Yes  3) No
explanation: The companyDescription output is produced and can be mapped for indexing (Yes). A WebApiSkill calls the external web API at the uri (Yes). The context '/document/organizations/*' uses a wildcard, so the skill runs for every organization, not just the first (No).
mlTitle: Custom Web API skill in AI Search

question:
You have the following data sources:
- Finance: On-premises Microsoft SQL Server database
- Sales: Azure Cosmos DB using the Core (SQL) API
- Logs: Azure Table storage
- HR: Azure SQL database

You need to ensure that you can search all the data by using the Azure Cognitive Search REST API.
What should you do?
options: A. Configure multiple read replicas for the data in Sales.
B. Mirror Finance to an Azure SQL database.
C. Ingest the data in Logs into Azure Data Explorer.
D. Ingest the data in Logs into Azure Sentinel.
correctText: B. Mirror Finance to an Azure SQL database.
explanation: Azure Cognitive Search indexers do not support on-premises SQL Server directly. Mirroring the Finance on-premises SQL Server data to an Azure SQL database makes it indexable, while Cosmos DB (SQL API), Table storage, and Azure SQL are already supported indexer sources.

question:
You are developing a solution to generate a word cloud based on the reviews of a company's products.
Which Text Analytics REST API endpoint should you use?
correctText: A. keyPhrases
explanation: Key phrase extraction returns the main talking points / important words and phrases from text, which is exactly what a word cloud of product reviews requires.

question:
DRAG DROP - You have a web app that uses Azure Cognitive Search. When reviewing billing for the app, you discover much higher than expected charges. You suspect that the query key is compromised. You need to prevent unauthorized access to the search endpoint and ensure that users only have read-only access to the documents collection. The solution must minimize app downtime. Which three actions should you perform in sequence?

Actions: Add a new query key; Regenerate the secondary admin key; Change the app to use the secondary admin key; Change the app to use the new key; Regenerate the primary admin key; Delete the compromised key.
order: Add a new query key
Change the app to use the new query key
Delete the compromised key.
correctText: 1) Add a new query key  2) Change the app to use the new query key  3) Delete the compromised key. (Query keys provide read-only access to the documents collection; rotating query keys avoids downtime and does not require regenerating admin keys.)
explanation: The compromised key is a query (read) key. Create a new query key, switch the app to it, then delete the compromised key. This restores read-only access with minimal downtime without touching admin keys.
mlTitle: Connect to Azure AI Search using API keys

question: You are developing an application that will use Azure Cognitive Search for internal documents. You need to implement document-level filtering for Azure Cognitive Search. Which three actions should you include in the solution? Each correct answer presents part of the solution.
options: A. Send Azure AD access tokens with the search request.
C. Retrieve the group memberships of the user.
D. Add allowed groups to each index entry.
E. Create one index per group.
F. Supply the groups as a filter for the search requests.
correctText: C. Retrieve the group memberships of the user; D. Add allowed groups to each index entry; F. Supply the groups as a filter for the search requests.
explanation: Document-level security is implemented by storing allowed group IDs on each document, retrieving the requesting user's group memberships, and applying those groups as an OData filter on the query.

question: You have an Azure Cognitive Search solution and an enrichment pipeline that performs Sentiment Analysis on social media posts. You need to define a knowledge store that will include the social media posts and the Sentiment Analysis results. Which two fields should you include in the definition? Each correct answer presents part of the solution.
correctText: B. storageConnectionString; E. objects
explanation: A knowledge store definition requires a storageConnectionString pointing to the Azure Storage account, and projections. To persist enriched JSON (posts plus sentiment results), object projections (objects) are used to write JSON blobs.
mlTitle: Knowledge store in Azure AI Search

question: SIMULATION - Task: You need to create an Azure resource named solution12345678 that will index a sample database named realestate-us-sample. The solution must ensure that users can search the index in English for people, organizations, and locations. To complete this task, sign in to the Azure portal.
correctText: Create an Azure AI Search (Cognitive Search) service named solution12345678, import the realestate-us-sample data using the Import data wizard, and in the cognitive skills step enable Entity Recognition / Extract people names, organization names, and location names with English as the language. This is a hands-on simulation with no multiple-choice answer.
explanation: An Azure AI Search service is provisioned and the built-in Import data wizard with cognitive enrichment (entity recognition skill for persons, organizations, locations) is used to index the sample real-estate database in English.
mlTitle: Add cognitive skills to Azure AI Search

question:
HOTSPOT - You create a knowledge store for Azure Cognitive Search by using the following JSON. The knowledgeStore.projections array contains two objects: the first object defines two table projections (unrelatedDocument and unrelatedKeyPhrases) with empty objects and files; the second object defines two object (blob) projections (unrelatedocrtext and unrelatedocrlayout) with empty tables and files. Complete each statement.

Statement 1: There will be [answer choice].
Statement 2: Normalized images will [answer choice].
options: Statement 1 choices: no projection groups | one projection group | two projection groups | four projection groups
Statement 2 choices: not be projected | be projected to Azure Blob storage | be projected to Azure File storage | be saved to an Azure Table storage
correctText: There will be TWO projection groups. Normalized images will BE PROJECTED TO AZURE BLOB storage.
explanation: Each object in the projections array is a separate projection group, and there are two objects, so there are two projection groups. The OCR/normalized-image content is defined under object projections (storageContainer), which writes JSON to Azure Blob storage.

question: You plan to create an index for an Azure Cognitive Search service by using the Azure portal. The Cognitive Search service will connect to an Azure SQL database. The Azure SQL database contains a table named UserMessages. Each row in UserMessages has a field named MessageCopy that contains the text of social media messages sent by a user. Users will perform full text searches against the MessageCopy field, and the values of the field will be shown to the users. You need to configure the properties of the index for the MessageCopy field to support the solution. Which attributes should you enable for the field?
correctText: D. Searchable and Retrievable
explanation: Full text search requires the Searchable attribute, and showing the field value to users requires the Retrievable attribute.
mlTitle: Create an index in Azure AI Search

question:
HOTSPOT - You have an Azure subscription that contains an Azure AI Document Intelligence resource named DI1. You create a PDF document named Test.pdf that contains tabular data. You need to analyze Test.pdf by using DI1. How should you complete the command?
